name: Topcoder Lookups API - Update with TypeScript, Prisma & PostgreSQL
description:
The current lookups-api provides reference data for Topcoder applications but relies on a complex architecture using DynamoDB and Elasticsearch. This creates maintenance challenges and makes local development difficult due to infrastructure dependencies.

We are revising the lookups-api to reduce complexity by migrating from the current stack to a modern TypeScript/Prisma/PostgreSQL architecture. The goal is to simplify development, improve maintainability, and retain all existing functionality.

## Required Links

- GitHub Repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/lookups-api

## Requirements

### Technology Migration
- Update the codebase to use TypeScript
- Replace Express with NestJS framework
- Replace DynamoDB with PostgreSQL
- Implement Prisma as the ORM
- Remove all Elasticsearch dependencies

### Schema Definition
- Create Prisma schema definitions for all existing models:
  - Country
  - Educational Institution
  - Device
- Ensure schema properly handles all fields from existing models
- Implement proper relationships between entities

### Data Migration
- Develop scripts to migrate data from DynamoDB to PostgreSQL
- Ensure all existing data is preserved during migration
- The data will be provided in the forum.

### API Functionality
- Maintain exact API endpoints and functionality
- Implement JWT authentication compatible with the existing system
- Preserve authorization rules for admin users and M2M clients
- Support the same query parameters for filtering and pagination
- Maintain consistent response formats

### Code Structure
- Follow NestJS best practices for project organization
- Implement proper separation of concerns (controllers, services, etc.)
- Use TypeScript interfaces and type safety throughout
- Implement proper error handling and validation

### Documentation
- Update API documentation with Swagger/OpenAPI
- Provide clear README with setup instructions
- Document the data migration process

### Testing
- Create seed data for testing purposes
- Ensure all existing functionality works as expected
- Write unit tests for key components

## Expected Deliverables
- Complete TypeScript/NestJS/Prisma codebase
- Prisma schema definitions
- Data migration scripts
- Updated API documentation
- README with setup instructions

## Submission Guidelines
- All source code for the updated application
- Migration scripts with documentation
- README.md with:
  - Setup instructions
  - Environment variable descriptions
  - Steps to run the application locally
  - Testing instructions
  - Migration process documentation
- Postman collection or equivalent API test suite

## Final Notes
- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarification if any requirements are unclear, do not make assumptions about functionality without approval
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code
- The winner will be required to make final fixes and push their submission to the designated repository
- Prisma schema and any related migration files should be well-documented

name: Topcoder Challenge API - Add field for "challengeSource"
description:
### Project overview

The current challenge  API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the challenge API to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

In order to achieve the above goal, we have updated the code in challenge [Topcoder Challenge  API: Update Code for Prisma Schema and updated Swagger](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/7dc44b10-2e27-4e57-bec1-4891c34aec4a) and have written Data migration tools in challenge [Topcoder Challenge API: Data Migration Tool to Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/22477223-2354-449d-a3b5-2d4d63c4cb25)

The goal for this challenge is to Add field for "challengeSource".

### Individual Requirements

We need a field to indicate the source of the challenge, like if it was launched from Work Manager, Topgear, Github, etc...

This would be an optional string text field (doesn't need to be an enum) at the root level of the challenge object. We can call it something like `challengeSource`.

Make sure this `challengeSource` is handled properly when creating/updating/searching/deleting challenge related APIs.

Please see: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/issues/9

### Code

- Challenge API code repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/tree/develop

### Unit tests

Unit tests are not in scope for this challenge, but any existing tests are expected to pass as they do now - we can't break any tests that are currently passing.

### Code quality
Please make sure you're following the code base quality & standards. Do not refactor/modify or start a new code styling. 

### Submission

Your submission should include:

* A patch file against the develop branch of Challenge API, hash a970a228f49d3a755855839a1416a3df1f079b9f or later
* A comprehensive README that covers the deployment and configuration of the code (if any configuration changes were required)
* A validation markdown file that covers how you tested the new project functionality

name: Topcoder Identity Service: tests implementation
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to write e2e tests, unit tests and postman collection tests.

## Detail Requirements

- Write E2E tests for each api based on old java project functionalities to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under `test/e2e` folder. 
- Update/Rewrite Unit tests to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under `test/unit` folder.
- Currently, there is only one postman test for each api which is not enough, thus, rewrite postman collections for each api to cover every cases.
- All tests should be targeting real database, not mock data. We have a data migration tool  which can be used to import data for tests.
- Log any bugs found during writing tests.
- Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to set up environment and database, how to migrate data, how to deploy locally, how to verify through postman collections;
- Make sure tests code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Source code
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

name: Projects API - Schema Definition and TypeScript Skeleton
description:
This challenge requires you to create the initial schema definition and TypeScript skeleton for a new version of the Topcoder Project Service. The existing service uses a combination of PostgreSQL and Elasticsearch, with a complex data model that includes many features that are no longer needed. This challenge focuses on designing a simplified schema and creating the initial TypeScript/NestJS application structure.

## Background

The current Topcoder Project Service manages projects and related data (members, attachments, etc.) using:
- Express.js backend
- PostgreSQL database
- Elasticsearch as a secondary data store
- Kafka for messaging
- Various deprecated features and complex data structures

We need to modernize this service by:
1. Moving to TypeScript, NestJS, and Prisma
2. Removing Elasticsearch dependency (PostgreSQL will be the only data store)
3. Simplifying the data model to focus only on essential features
4. Flattening the schema to avoid JSON columns for better query performance
5. Streamlining Kafka messaging to only include necessary events

## Required Links

- Existing Projects API: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-project-service](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-project-service)

## Individual Requirements

### 1. Database Schema Design

Design a PostgreSQL schema using Prisma that:

- Flattens the current data structure to avoid JSON columns
- Includes only the necessary entities (see below)
- Properly defines relationships between entities
- Includes appropriate indexes for performance
- Supports all required queries

#### Required Entities:

1. **Project**
   - Core project information (id, name, description, status, etc.)
   - Flatten relevant fields from the current **details** JSON column
   - Include only necessary fields (see example project JSON in challenge forum)

2. **ProjectMember**
   - User association with projects
   - Role information
   - Primary flag

3. **ProjectAttachment**
   - File/link attachments for projects

4. **ProjectMemberInvite**
   - Invitation records for project members

### 2. NestJS Application Structure

Create a TypeScript/NestJS application structure that includes:

- Properly organized modules following NestJS best practices
- Prisma service integration
- Authentication module (JWT-based)
- Basic controller structure for all required endpoints
- Service layer with business logic interfaces
- DTO definitions for all API requests/responses
- Kafka integration for required events

### 3. API Compatibility

**IMPORTANT**: The new service must maintain API compatibility with the existing project service. This means:

- All existing API endpoints must be preserved with the same URL patterns
- Request and response formats must remain the same
- Query parameters must be supported as in the current implementation
- Pagination, sorting, and filtering capabilities must be maintained
- Authentication and authorization mechanisms must work the same way

This requirement ensures that consumers of the API will not need to modify their integration when the new service is deployed.

### 4. Messaging Integration

**IMPORTANT**: Use the bus API for all Kafka message publishing. Instead of directly integrating with Kafka, all event publishing should be done through the bus API service.

Implement the integration for the following events only:

```
PROJECT_MEMBER_ADDED: 'project.member.added'
PROJECT_MEMBER_REMOVED: 'project.member.removed'
PROJECT_MEMBER_UPDATED: 'project.member.updated'

PROJECT_ATTACHMENT_ADDED: 'project.attachment.added'
PROJECT_ATTACHMENT_REMOVED: 'project.attachment.removed'
PROJECT_ATTACHMENT_UPDATED: 'project.attachment.updated'

PROJECT_UPDATED: 'project.updated'
PROJECT_DELETED: 'project.deleted'

PROJECT_MEMBER_INVITE_CREATED: 'project.member.invite.created'
PROJECT_MEMBER_INVITE_UPDATED: 'project.member.invite.updated'
PROJECT_MEMBER_INVITE_REMOVED: 'project.member.invite.deleted'

PROJECT_CREATED: 'project.action.create'
PROJECT_UPDATED: 'project.action.update'
PROJECT_DELETED: 'project.action.delete'
PROJECT_BILLING_ACCOUNT_UPDATED: 'project.action.billingAccount.update'
```

### 5. Email Integration

Implement direct email sending via the bus API and the **external.action.email** topic, replacing the current convoluted notification process. This should follow the same pattern as the event publishing described in the Messaging Integration section.

## Final Notes

1. This challenge is the first step in modernizing the TC Project Service. The focus should be on creating a clean, efficient foundation that can be built upon in subsequent challenges. The design should prioritize simplicity and maintainability while ensuring all current functionality is preserved.
2. Follow NestJS best practices for module organization
3. Implement proper error handling
4. Include TypeScript types/interfaces for all models
5. **API compatibility is critical** - the new service must be a drop-in replacement for the existing one from an API consumer perspective
6. Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.

## Submission Guidelines

A NestJS application that includes

1. **Prisma Schema File**
   - Complete **schema.prisma** with all required models
   - Comments explaining design decisions

2. **NestJS Application Structure**
   - Complete folder structure following NestJS conventions
   - Module definitions
   - Controller skeletons with endpoints matching existing API
   - Service interfaces
   - DTO definitions that maintain compatibility with current request/response formats

3. **Bus API Integration**
   - Implementation of the bus API service for event publishing
   - Event payload definitions for all required events
   - Email sending integration using the same bus API service

4. **Documentation**
   - Schema design documentation explaining the data model
   - API endpoint documentation showing compatibility with existing endpoints
   - Explanation of changes from the original service
   - Deployment guide

name: Topcoder NestJS Autopilot - Event-Based Phase Transition Fixes
description:
In [the previous challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30377091/?type=develop), we implemented an event-based mechanism for the Topcoder Autopilot service to automatically transition challenges through their phases at appropriate times. A code review has identified several issues that need to be fixed to ensure the system works as intended. This First2Finish challenge focuses on addressing these issues to improve the reliability and maintainability of the autopilot service.

### Technology Stack

- NestJS 10+
- TypeScript 5+
- @nestjs/schedule module
- Kafka
- ESLint and Prettier for code formatting
- Docker

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git

### Core Requirements

1. **Fix ChallengeUpdatePayload and CommandPayload Interfaces**

   The current implementation modified the **ChallengeUpdatePayload** and **CommandPayload** interfaces to include phase-related fields, which breaks compatibility with existing systems. Refactor the code to:
   
   - Restore the original interfaces without phase-specific fields
   - Create a mock API service to fetch phase details when needed
   - Ensure backward compatibility with existing message formats

2. **Implement Individual Phase Cancellation**

   The current **cancel_schedule** command cancels all phases for a given project, even when a specific phase ID is provided. Update the implementation to:
   
   - Respect the **phaseId** parameter in the **cancel_schedule** command
   - Cancel only the specified phase when a **phaseId** is provided
   - Maintain the ability to cancel all phases when no **phaseId** is specified

3. **Handle Past Dates Properly**

   The current implementation throws an error when trying to schedule a phase transition with a date in the past. Update the code to:
   
   - Process phase transitions with past dates immediately instead of throwing an error
   - Maintain consistent behavior between recovery and regular processing
   - Ensure the system can handle events from the Challenge API after it recovers from a crash

4. **Fix Log Messages**

   The current log messages are confusing and sometimes misleading. Improve the logging by:
   
   - Only logging "Successfully rescheduled phase" when an existing job was actually rescheduled
   - Replacing "Canceled scheduled transition" with more appropriate wording when cleaning up after successful execution
   - Using consistent terminology throughout the logging system

5. **Fix Return Values in cancelScheduledTransition**

   The **cancelScheduledTransition** method always returns **true**, even when no job was found to cancel. Update the method to:
   
   - Return **false** when no job is found to cancel
   - Return **true** only when a job was actually canceled
   - Ensure consistent behavior in error cases

6. **Add Challenge ID to Phase Transition Payload**

   The current payload published to **autopilot.phase.transition** does not include a separate challenge ID field. Update the implementation to:
   
   - Include a distinct **challengeId** field in the payload
   - Maintain backward compatibility with existing consumers
   - Ensure the payload meets the requirements specified in the challenge spec

7. **Define Command Constants**

   The commands like **cancel_schedule** and **reschedule_phase** are currently hardcoded. Refactor the code to:
   
   - Define these commands as constants in a **config.constants.ts** file
   - Use the constants throughout the codebase
   - Document the available commands and their parameters

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the develop branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git, which should include:

1. Complete implementation of all the fixes listed above

name: Topcoder NestJS Autopilot - Challenge API Integration
description:
The Topcoder Autopilot service is responsible for automatically transitioning challenges through their phases at the appropriate times. In the first challenge, we established the core project infrastructure and integrated with Kafka for event messaging. The second challenge implemented an event-based mechanism for phase transitions. This third challenge focuses on integrating the Autopilot service with the Challenge API to monitor and manage phase transitions more effectively.

### Technology Stack

- NestJS 10+
- TypeScript 5+
- @nestjs/schedule module
- Kafka
- REST API integration
- ESLint and Prettier for code formatting
- Docker

### Challenge Background

The current Autopilot-v6 codebase has established the Kafka infrastructure and implemented event-based scheduling for phase transitions. However, it lacks direct integration with the Challenge API, which is necessary for monitoring phase end times and managing transitions for all active challenges. The existing **ChallengeAPIService** in **autopilot-v6/src/challenge/challenge-api.service.ts** only contains a mock implementation that needs to be replaced with a real integration to the Challenge API.

### Codebase

- Autopilot Service: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git)
- Challenge API: 
    - Source code: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6.git)
    - Hosted version: [https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/challenges](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/challenges)

**Important Note**: This challenge requires modifications ONLY to the autopilot-v6 codebase. The challenge-api-v6 codebase should NOT be modified and should be treated as an external service to be integrated with.

### Individual Requirements

#### 1. Phase End Time Monitoring Service

Implement a service to interact with the Challenge API by replacing the mock implementation of the existing **ChallengeAPIService**:

- Complete the implementation of the Challenge API client service to retrieve challenge and phase information
- Develop methods to fetch all active challenges and their corresponding phase details
- Implement filtering capabilities to focus on challenges with upcoming phase transitions
- Create mechanisms to detect new challenges and modifications to existing challenges

#### 2. Challenge and Phase Registration

Enhance the Autopilot service to register and track challenges:

- Add Kafka consumers to subscribe to the **challenge.notification.create** topic from Challenge API
- When new challenges are created, automatically retrieve and register their phase details
- Calculate phase end times based on the Challenge API data
- Register these end times with the scheduler service implemented in the previous challenge
- Implement proper error handling and retry mechanisms for API failures

#### 3. Challenge Update Handling

Implement logic to handle changes to challenges and their phases:

- Add Kafka consumers to subscribe to the **challenge.notification.update** topic from Challenge API
- Create handlers for challenge update events from Kafka
- When challenge details are modified, update the corresponding phase schedules
- Handle edge cases like immediate phase transitions, phase cancellations, or challenge cancellations
- Ensure all changes are properly logged for audit purposes

#### 4. Comprehensive Integration

Develop a complete integration between the Autopilot service and Challenge API:

- Implement regular synchronization to ensure Autopilot's view of challenges stays current
- Create a recovery mechanism that can reconcile differences between scheduled transitions and actual challenge states
- Implement proper validation of challenge data to prevent scheduling errors
- Ensure the system handles API throttling and rate limiting appropriately

#### 5. Documentation

- Document the Challenge API integration approach
- Include examples of how the system handles different scenarios (new challenge creation, challenge updates, etc.)
- Provide a deployment guide for the integrated system

### Implementation Details

#### Kafka Events to Subscribe

The following Kafka events from the Challenge API should be subscribed to:

1. **challenge.notification.create** - Emitted when a new challenge is created
2. **challenge.notification.update** - Emitted when a challenge is updated

The autopilot-v6 service should be extended to consume these events in addition to the existing topics it already handles:
- **autopilot.phase.transition**
- **autopilot.challenge.update**
- **autopilot.command**

#### Challenge API Endpoints to Integrate

The following Challenge API endpoints should be utilized for integration:

1. **/challenges** (GET) - To fetch all active challenges with filtering options
   - Use query parameters like **status=ACTIVE** to get only active challenges
   - Use **isLightweight=true** to get optimized responses for bulk fetching

2. **/challenges/:challengeId** (GET) - To get detailed information about a specific challenge

3. **/challenges/:challengeId/advance-phase** (POST) - To trigger phase transitions

4. **/challenge-phases** (GET) - To get information about available challenge phases

5. **/challenge-phases/:challengePhaseId** (GET) - To get detailed information about a specific phase

The implementation should include proper authentication and error handling for all API calls.

#### Challenge API Client Service

Replace the mock implementation in the existing **ChallengeAPIService** with a real implementation that:

1. Interfaces with the Challenge API to retrieve challenge and phase information
2. Provides methods to:
   - Fetch all active challenges using appropriate filtering
   - Get detailed phase information for a specific challenge
   - Monitor for challenge updates and modifications
   - Handle pagination and bulk data retrieval efficiently

Example interface (Expanding on the existing service, only for your reference):

```typescript
interface ChallengeApiService {
  getActiveChallenge(challengeId: string): Promise<ChallengeDto>;
  getAllActiveChallenges(filters?: ChallengeFiltersDto): Promise<ChallengeDto[]>;
  getChallengePhases(challengeId: string): Promise<PhaseDto[]>;
  getPhaseDetails(challengeId: number, phaseId: number): Promise<IPhase | null>;
  getPhaseTypeName(challengeId: number, phaseId: number): Promise<string>;
  advancePhase(challengeId: string, phaseId: string, operation: string): Promise<PhaseAdvanceResponseDto>;
}
```

#### Challenge Registration Handler

Enhance the existing AutopilotService to:

1. Process new challenge creation events
2. Extract phase information and calculate end times
3. Register phase transitions with the scheduler service
4. Handle challenge update events and modify scheduled transitions accordingly

#### Synchronization Mechanism

Implement a synchronization service that:

1. Runs on a configurable schedule
2. Fetches all active challenges and their phases from the Challenge API
3. Compares this data with the currently scheduled transitions
4. Adds missing transitions and removes obsolete ones
5. Provides detailed logs of synchronization activities

### Important Notes

1. The existing Kafka infrastructure and scheduler service should be leveraged for this integration
2. All API calls should implement proper error handling, retries, and circuit breaking
3. The solution should be designed with scalability in mind, handling potentially thousands of active challenges
4. Proper error handling and logging are essential for debugging and monitoring
5. All required features must be properly implemented
6. Only modify the autopilot-v6 codebase. Do not modify the challenge-api-v6 codebase.
7. Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.

### Submission Guideline

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the develop branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git), which should include:

1. Complete implementation of the Challenge API integration
2. Enhanced phase monitoring and registration mechanisms
3. Comprehensive synchronization and recovery procedures
4. Documentation on the integration approach and deployment guide (in markdown format)

name: Topcoder Member API - Add in stats and history endpoints
description:
## Project Overview
The current member  API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the resources API to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

This challenge focuses on **adding new endpoints to member API**. 

## Requirements
Following endpoints are in scope:
1. POST /members/:handle/stats/history  create stats history
2. PATCH /members/:handle/stats/history partially update stats history
3. POST /members/:handle/stats create member stats.
4. PATCH /members/:handle/stats partially update stats

#### Update Operation
As there is no delete operation for history item and stats item, so it will pass by all the sub-items like memberDevelopStatsItem in update payload.
1. For the items which has id, update the mutated fields.
2. For the items which has no id, create a new record for this item.
3. For the items which existed in db, but not existed in the payload, delete them from the database.

#### Code Reorganization
1. Add controller to manage stats history data.
- GET /members/:handle/stats/history
- POST /members/:handle/stats/history
- PATCH /members/:handle/stats/history

2. Add controller to manage stats data.
- GET /members/:handle/stats
- POST /members/:handle/stats
- PATCH /members/:handle/stats

#### General Requirements
-  Setup test data locally to test the APIs and make sure test data are enough and data returned match the exist APIs deployed here https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v5/members (Note: this will be considered as a **MAJOR** requirement)
-  For setting up test data, you can download the data from https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v5/members as JSONs and then have a separate script to load that data into the test DB;
-  Update postman collections properly if they're obsolete;
-  Update the Readme on how to configure, run and verify the code;

NOTE: Ask questions in the forum if anything is unclear —do not assume functionality!
## Technology Stack

- Backend: NodeJS 
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger
- Postgres version **16.3**

## Resources
- Revisited API code: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6
- Old Member API code for reference: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop

## Unit tests
Any existing tests are expected to pass as they do now - we can't break any tests that are currently passing.

## Submission Guidelines

✅ A patch file against latest commit 632531cd647b1eb1d885c1a04d523e10dd19b60a on [develop branch](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6/tree/develop) 
✅ Seed data for testing: Any required data for testing and validating your submission should be included.
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and test details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .
- Winner will need to **do final fix** and push his submission to new repository.

name: Learn AI – Build and Deploy an AI Agent with Topcoder MCP on Hugging Face
description:
Welcome to the Learn AI Challenge series\! Your mission is to explore AI and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) by building an innovative AI Agent application that leverages the Topcoder MCP server.

The agent can take many forms—chatbot, automation tool, research assistant, code generator, data visualization tool, or something entirely unique.

You are free to use open-source agents or develop your own from scratch. The only requirement is that the agent must utilize the Topcoder MCP server and deliver a meaningful, user-friendly experience through the server's tools and resources.

This is your opportunity to learn, innovate, and demonstrate your creativity and engineering expertise using cutting-edge AI technologies—plus earn rewards and community recognition.

## **Challenge Incentives**

* Top 10 winners will receive cash prizes.
* Winners and top submissions will be awarded an "AI Explorer" badge on their Topcoder profiles.
* Selected high-quality submissions will be featured on the [Topcoder Hugging Face organization page](https://huggingface.co/Topcoder-Platform), providing recognition and exposure to the broader AI community.

## **Challenge Details**

**Technology Focus:** Development of AI Agents using the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
**Submission Duration:** 21 days
**Agent Type:** Any form of intelligent application is allowed—such as chatbots, virtual assistants, research tools, code generators, or data visualization agents. 
**Creativity:** Participants are encouraged to push boundaries and design original, inventive AI applications.
**Functionality:** The AI agent must address a clearly defined problem or purpose and deliver impactful, practical results.
**Implementation Requirements:**
* The AI agent must be designed to run within a [Hugging Face Space](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/spaces-overview).
* You can use any SDK or programming language, as long as it functions correctly in the Hugging Face Space environment.
* The agent must run smoothly on the default free-tier hardware (CPU Basic) provided by Hugging Face—no GPU usage required.
* It must connect to and interact with the Topcoder MCP server and deliver clear, purposeful results through an intuitive and user-friendly interface.

## **Submission Requirements**

* A fully functional AI Agent application, either as a downloadable codebase (zip file) or a publicly accessible Hugging Face Space link—or both.
* The submission must be based on a clearly defined use case that addresses a specific problem. Participants should also include a simple, clear document (as part of the submission) that outlines the problem being solved, the rationale behind the chosen use case, and how the AI agent addresses it. This clarity should also be reflected in the agent’s implementation and user experience.
* Accompanying documentation that clearly explains how to deploy, configure, and run the AI agent on Hugging Face Spaces.
* An optional demo video (up to 5 minutes) showcasing the agent’s core functionality and how it addresses the defined use case.

## **What You Will Learn**

By participating in this challenge, you will:

* Gain a practical understanding of AI agent architecture, development workflows, and prompt engineering techniques.
* Strengthen your hands-on skills using technologies such as TypeScript, Python, JSON, APIs, and Gradio in real-world scenarios.
* Learn to build, integrate, and test AI applications using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server provided by Topcoder.
* Develop proficiency in working with data ingestion, backend logic, and designing clear and effective user experiences (UI/UX) for AI tools.
* Learn how to prepare, optimize, and deploy your application to Hugging Face Spaces using their CPU Basic environment.
* Improve your ability to define clear use cases, articulate the problem your solution addresses, and communicate that effectively through documentation and demonstration.
* Engage with the broader Topcoder AI community to gain visibility, collect feedback, and earn recognition through badges, publications, or prizes.
* Build portfolio-worthy work that demonstrates your ability to solve meaningful problems using modern AI tools.

## **Learning Path & Plan**

This 3-week challenge is structured as a guided learning journey. Below is a comprehensive, curriculum-style blueprint divided into modules, each with a clear objective, tasks, and deliverables. This will help you progress from beginner to deployer by the end of the challenge.

### **Module 1: Foundations of AI Agents & MCP (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Understand the core principles of AI agents and how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables real-world AI integration.

* What are AI agents? (Examples: chatbots, automation tools, assistants, research agents)
* Core capabilities of agents: statefulness, prompt context, responsiveness, and output generation
* Overview of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and how it facilitates structured communication between users and AI models
* Learn about transports like SSE and Streamable HTTP used by MCP
* Understand how MCP connects to Topcoder’s public APIs and what kind of tools/data you can access

* **Recommended Course:** [Model Context Protocol (MCP) Course by Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/learn/mcp-course/unit0/introduction#welcome-to-the--model-context-protocol-mcp-course)

  * Covers how to build MCP clients
  * Shows integration with Gradio
  * Includes code walkthroughs, examples, and deployment steps

* **Deliverable:** Written summary explaining:  
  * The type of AI agent you want to build  
  * How MCP can be used in that context

### **Module 2: Development Environment Setup (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Get your local environment ready to build, test, and run MCP-powered AI agents.

* Install and configure a suitable IDE (e.g., VSCode, Cursor)
* Set up the Topcoder MCP server endpoint (`https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp`) in your IDE’s `settings.json`
* Install relevant SDKs and dependencies (e.g., TypeScript, Python, Gradio, Node.js, etc.)
* Use the MCP Inspector to validate server connection and test a basic query

**Recommended Resources:**

* [Gradio Getting Started Guide (with code examples and UI integrations)](https://www.gradio.app/guides)
* [VSCode Official Setup Instructions](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs), [Cursor Editor (AI-powered IDE)](https://www.cursor.so/)
* [Topcoder MCP Inspector GitHub](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector)

**Deliverable:**

* A working development environment
* Screenshot or confirmation of successful test interaction with MCP server using MCP Inspector

### **Module 3: Exploring MCP Server Capabilities (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Learn how to explore the Topcoder MCP server, understand available tools and data, and identify what can power your agent.

* Understand the difference between SSE and Streamable HTTP transports in MCP.
* Launch the MCP Inspector and connect it to the Topcoder MCP endpoint (`https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp`).
* Explore the public tools and resources available via the server.
* Review example requests and responses, and document the ones relevant to your intended use case.
* Determine the input and output formats for at least one useful endpoint.

**Recommended Resources:**

* [Topcoder MCP Inspector GitHub](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector)
* [SSE and HTTP Streaming \- MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events)

**Deliverable:**

* A table listing at least 3 MCP tools or endpoints with their purpose, input, and expected output
* Screenshots or logs of actual request/response payloads from the MCP Inspector

### **Module 4: Refine the Use Case & Design the Agent (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Revisit your original agent idea after exploring the Topcoder MCP server and finalize a well-defined use case and experience flow.

* Reflect on your initial idea from Module 1 in light of the MCP tools and data you've explored
* Validate whether the original use case still makes sense or needs to be adapted
* Clearly define:
  * The real-world problem you're solving
  * Why it's meaningful
  * How your agent will solve it using one or more MCP tools
* Create a user journey flow, wireframe, or basic mockup to visualize how users will interact with your agent

**Deliverable:**

* Finalized use case document with:
  * Problem definition
  * Target user or audience
  * Description of solution and value proposition
  * MCP tools or endpoints your agent will use
* UX mockup or user journey diagram that reflects the agent's intended experience. You can use [v0.dev](https://v0.dev/) to quickly generate visually appealing mockups with the help of AI.

### **Module 5: Building Agent Logic & MCP Integration (Days 2–3)**

**Objective:** Implement the core logic of your agent to communicate effectively with the Topcoder MCP server, retrieve data, and generate intelligent outputs.

* Set up your project structure using a preferred language (Python, TypeScript, etc.)
* Choose your connection method (SSE or Streamable HTTP) and implement a request handler for MCP
* Integrate the MCP endpoint(s) you selected in Module 3
* Process incoming data and prepare meaningful responses
* Apply prompt engineering principles to enhance agent output quality
* Ensure your logic runs efficiently on Hugging Face CPU Basic (no GPU dependencies)

**Recommended Resources:**

* [Prompt Engineering Guide](https://github.com/dair-ai/Prompt-Engineering-Guide)
* Example backend agent using Python with SSE \+ Hugging Face: [MCP Agent Boilerplate (Python)](https://github.com/lastmile-ai/mcp-agent)
  * Demonstrates how to:
    * Connect to Topcoder MCP using SSE
    * Build an async backend agent using FastAPI
    * Deploy to Hugging Face with CPU basic config
  * This is ideal if you're building a backend-only agent with no frontend SDK involved.
* EventSource API Reference: [MDN Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/EventSource) Guide\]([https://github.com/dair-ai/Prompt-Engineering-Guide](https://github.com/dair-ai/Prompt-Engineering-Guide))
* Example SSE integration (React or Node): [MDN EventSource Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/EventSource)

**Deliverable:**

* A backend agent logic module that:
  * Successfully connects to and queries the MCP server
  * Returns usable responses to the frontend
  * Demonstrates use of at least one real MCP tool or endpoint
* A brief explanation (in your README) of how the MCP connection is implemented and which tools it uses

### **Module 6: UI/UX Development (Days 2–3)**

**Objective:** Create an accessible, intuitive interface to showcase your AI agent’s capabilities.

* Choose a simple UI framework such as Gradio, Streamlit, or a minimal HTML/JS frontend (Gradio recommended for ease of Hugging Face integration)
* Link the UI to your backend logic (built in Module 5\)
* Focus on making input fields and output displays clear and accessible
* Provide contextual labels, input placeholders, and output formatting where helpful
* Consider adding a loading indicator or progress feedback if MCP requests take time
* 
**Deliverable:** A lightweight, working UI prototype that allows users to interact with your agent and see live responses from the MCP server

### **Module 7: End-to-End Testing & Debugging (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Ensure your agent is production-ready through comprehensive testing and optimization.

* Perform full end-to-end testing: UI → backend → MCP → backend → UI
* Simulate real user interactions and input variations
* Identify and handle edge cases, unexpected input, and communication errors (e.g., timeouts, SSE disconnects)
* Add fallback behavior or helpful messages for errors
* Profile and optimize response time, agent feedback, and loading states

**Deliverable:** A stable, polished, and bug-free agent that reliably handles diverse inputs and edge scenarios

### **Module 8: Deployment on Hugging Face Spaces (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Deploy your agent to a live environment and ensure it's accessible and stable.

* Prepare `requirements.txt` and `README.md` with necessary dependencies and setup instructions
* Push your project to a new or existing Hugging Face Space
* Confirm successful deployment on the default CPU Basic hardware (no GPU)
* Perform smoke testing to validate end-to-end functionality

**Deliverable:** A fully deployed Hugging Face Space (public link) or a repository that’s ready to be deployed without additional changes

### **Module 9: Documentation & Demo (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Ensure your project is easy to understand, use, and present.

* Create a well-structured README that includes setup instructions, usage examples, and technical details
* Clearly articulate the use case and the problem your agent addresses
* (Optional) Record a 3–5 minute demo video walking through how your agent works

**Deliverable:** Comprehensive documentation with an optional demo video

### **Module 10: Final Packaging & Submission (Day 1\)**

**Objective:** Finalize and submit your agent project with all required materials.

* Package your project as a zip file or provide a live public link (e.g., Hugging Face Space)
* Carefully review the challenge submission checklist to ensure everything is included
* Submit your entry via the challenge platform before the deadline

**Deliverable:** A complete submission package that meets all criteria and is ready for judging

## **The Topcoder MCP Server**

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) server provided by Topcoder is hosted at: [https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp).

It supports two transport mechanisms for agent communication:
* **Server-Sent Events (SSE):** [https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/sse](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/sse)
* **Streamable HTTP:** [https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp)

Your agent must use one of these transports to interact with the MCP server.
No authentication is currently required—your agent can connect and send requests without needing a token.
The server exposes publicly available data and tools sourced from the Topcoder APIs. In future updates, we may introduce authenticated features and enhanced tools that require login or user tokens.

## **Review & Evaluation**

We will review submissions on an **iterative basis**. After completing each module, please upload your work (e.g., Document, link to GitHub repo, Hugging Face Space, or zipped files) and notify the challenge copilot by email, the copilot will reivew and share feedback over email.

You will typically receive feedback within **1–2 days**, helping you improve and refine your work before progressing.

This **optional collaborative review** process ensures you stay on track and gives you the best chance of submitting a high-quality agent by the end.

## **Final Evaluation Criteria**

Your final submission will be judged based on the following:

* **Functionality & Stability (30%)** – Does the agent work as intended across different inputs and edge cases?
* **Use Case Relevance (20%)** – How clearly defined and meaningful is the problem the agent addresses?
* **MCP Integration (20%)** – How effectively does the agent leverage the Topcoder MCP tools and transports?
* **User Experience & Interface (15%)** – Is the UI intuitive, responsive, and polished?
* **Documentation & Demo (10%)** – Is the README clear? Does the optional video explain the agent effectively?
* **Originality & Creativity (5%)** – Is the idea novel or uniquely executed?

High-scoring submissions typically demonstrate smooth functionality, a clear purpose, practical use of MCP tools, and thoughtful design.

name: Topcoder Identity Service: fix authentication issue
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookuap roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're going to fix authentication issue presented in the new code.

## Detail Requirements

1. Fix the authentication issue:
 We find a request to authenticate a known user in dev (with the v6 service configured with the same blowfish key as the legacy service) doesn't work.
 If I encrypt a plain text password using the configuration in the Identity service (UserService.encodePasswordLegacy), using the same key as the legacy service, I get a different result than what's stored in the database for the users.
When do authentication, the new code tries to decrypt the password in the DB to compare against the plain text password coming in. This logic is inconsistent with original logic.
See the logic in the new code https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/blob/0c41db577fb184589bd48022be6877e0e42e25e5/src/api/user/auth-flow.service.ts#L1009-L1010
and compare that with logic in the old code https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/blob/c5d95c78ef6e50eca64e2f808ac10fc075fa0b4f/src/main/java/com/appirio/tech/core/service/identity/dao/UserDAO.java#L817-L818

 Basicially, we should be consistent with the old code, which is shown as follows:
 1. Get the current, encrypted, password from the DB
 2. Encrypt the provided password using Blowfish
 3. Compare the encrypted provided password with the encrypted password from the DB
 4. If they match, allow login, otherwise reject

The above issue exists in the following methods which need to be fixed and test through postman tests:
 - user.controller.ts#getOneTimeToken: the logic should be matching https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/blob/c5d95c78ef6e50eca64e2f808ac10fc075fa0b4f/src/main/java/com/appirio/tech/core/service/identity/resource/UserResource.java#L2036
 - user.controller.ts#auth0Login : the logic should be matching https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/blob/c5d95c78ef6e50eca64e2f808ac10fc075fa0b4f/src/main/java/com/appirio/tech/core/service/identity/resource/UserResource.java#L788

2. Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to verify through postman collections;
3. Make sure tests code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Source code
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

name: Topcoder Identity Service: feature improvements
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to use single Postgres schema and remove group related tables.

## Detail Requirements

- Remove the dependency of `Crypto.JS` as it's depcrecated, and pease use `crypto`. There are mainly two services involved: `UserService`, and `AuthFlowService`. see details here https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/issues/4
- Remove `Group` and `GroupMembership` tables, instead using Group Service v6. Details can be seen here https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/issues/3
- Remove `common_oltp` and `authorization` schemas and use a single `identity` schema. See https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/issues/1
- Currently, there is only one postman test for each api which is not enough, thus, rewrite postman collections for each api to cover every cases. This will be considered as a **MAJOR REQUIREMENT**.
- Changes made should be tested thouroughly, so that the functionalities are consistent with the old java code.
- Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to set up environment and database, how to migrate data, how to deploy locally, how to verify through postman collections;
- Make sure code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.
- Tests are out of scope.

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

Topcoder Review API - Legacy Submissions Data Import for Submissions API Migration - REPOST
description:
This challenge focuses on migrating historical submissions data from Topcoder's legacy system to the new Prisma-based schema in the Topcoder Review API. In [the previous challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30377016/?type=develop), we successfully migrated the Submissions API endpoints into the Review API while maintaining backward compatibility. Now, we need to import the legacy submissions data to ensure these endpoints return real data instead of mock responses.

The data to be imported comes from two sources:
1. Legacy Informix database exports (JSON format)
2. ElasticSearch exports from the current Submissions API

### Technology Stack

- **Backend**: NestJS
- **ORM**: Prisma with PostgreSQL
- **ID Format**: nanoID (to maintain compatibility with the Review API)
- **API Documentation**: OpenAPI/Swagger
- **Package Manager**: pnpm

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api)
- Prisma Schema: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma)

### Core Requirements:

#### 1. Data Transformation Rules

**1.1 Submissions (Informix):**

- Convert legacy submission records to the new Prisma schema format
- Map submission status IDs to the appropriate enum values
- Map submission type IDs to the appropriate enum values
- Preserve all metadata including scores, placements, and timestamps
- Ensure proper relationships between submissions and related entities (uploads, users, challenges)

**1.2 Uploads (Informix):**

- Transform upload records to the new schema format
- Map upload status IDs to appropriate enum values
- Map upload type IDs to appropriate enum values
- Maintain relationships between uploads and submissions

**1.3 Submission Artifacts (Informix):**

- Import submission artifacts data if available
- Ensure proper relationships between submissions and their artifacts

**1.4 ElasticSearch Data:**

- Import data from ElasticSearch exports (dev-submissions-api.data.json)
- Map ElasticSearch document fields to the appropriate Prisma schema fields
- Handle different document types (reviews, submissions, etc.)
- Preserve all metadata and relationships

**1.5 Review Migration Error Fix**

- Fix the review migration errors, particularly for review records with undefined error codes

```
[review][review_1.json] Error code: undefined, LegacyId: 48166
[review][review_1.json] Error code: undefined, LegacyId: 48167
[review][review_1.json] Error code: undefined, LegacyId: 48168
[review][review_1.json] Error code: undefined, LegacyId: 48169
```

And improve the error message to make it more readable.

#### 2. Data Integration Rules

**2.1 Handling Overlapping Data:**

- All submissions in ElasticSearch should have a legacy ID that corresponds to a record in the Informix data
- Not all Informix data will have a corresponding record in ElasticSearch
- When overlaps exist, merge the data appropriately:
  - Use ElasticSearch data for newer fields/properties not present in Informix
  - Use Informix data as the authoritative source for core fields
  - Preserve unique identifiers from both systems to maintain backward compatibility

**2.2 ID Mapping:**

- Create a mapping between legacy IDs and new nanoIDs
- Ensure all relationships are properly maintained during the migration
- Preserve legacy IDs in the new schema for backward compatibility

#### 3. Validation Requirements

**3.1 Data Quality Checks:**

- Verify all enum mappings against lookup tables (submission_status_lu, submission_type_lu, etc.)
- Ensure required fields contain valid values
- Validate relationships between submissions and uploads
- Confirm data integrity across all imported records
- Verify that ElasticSearch and Informix data are properly integrated

**3.2 API Response Validation:**

- All Submissions API endpoints must return actual database records
- POST/PUT/PATCH operations must persist to migrated data
- Pagination must work with large datasets
- Filter parameters must produce correct results
- Download endpoints must function correctly with the imported data


### Submission Guidelines

Please submit:

1. A git patch against the feat/submissions-api branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git), which should include:
   - Updated Prisma schema if necessary (get approval before making changes)
   - Data migration scripts for both Informix and ElasticSearch data
   - Any necessary modifications to existing services to work with the imported data
   - Tests to verify data integrity and API functionality
   - Error handling mechanisms for migration failures

2. Data Migration Script
   - Provide scripts for migrating both Informix and ElasticSearch data to the new system
   - Use the official [Prisma seeding](https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/prisma-migrate/workflows/seeding) workflow for data imports
   - JSON-to-Prisma Transformation Logic:
     - Implement logic to transform both data sources into Prisma-compatible formats
     - Handle data merging when records exist in both sources
   - Batch Import with Progress Tracking:
     - Enable batch data imports with a mechanism to track progress
     - Handle the large volume of data efficiently
   - Rollback Mechanism for Failed Imports:
     - Ensure a rollback mechanism is in place to handle failed imports

3. Validation Suite
   - Postman collection testing all Submissions API endpoints with imported data
   - A document in markdown format with data consistency checks results between legacy/new systems
   - Tests to verify proper integration of data from both sources
   - Tests specifically validating the handling of problematic review records

4. Documentation (in markdown format)
   - Data mapping specification document detailing how fields from both sources map to the new schema
   - Deployment guide
   - Data reconciliation guide explaining how overlaps between data sources were handled

### Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Use the given [GitHub repo's develop branch](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api) as the code base.
- Don't break any existing functionalities of the codebase.
- Ensure Swagger docs are accessible at http://localhost:3000/api-docs.
- The goal is to have all Submissions API endpoints return real data from the migrated legacy system while maintaining backward compatibility.
- Remember that all submissions in ElasticSearch should have a legacy ID that corresponds to a record in the Informix data, but not all Informix data will have a corresponding record in ElasticSearch.

name: PieceFinder Computer Vision Proof of Concept Challenge
description:
# Challenge Overview

Welcome to the **PieceFinder Computer Vision Proof of Concept Challenge**!  
In this challenge, you will help shape the technical foundation of PieceFinder, an innovative app for puzzle enthusiasts. PieceFinder aims to assist users in finding specific puzzle pieces and counting all pieces using state-of-the-art computer vision and AI.  
Your mission is to research, prototype, and demonstrate core technologies that will power the app’s puzzle-solving features.

---

## Background

A customer is creating an app called **Piece Finder** or **PieceFinder**.  
The app is for puzzle lovers and the main function is to assist puzzlers when they are stuck trying to find a specific piece of a puzzle.  

For many who do physical puzzles, they enjoy the quiet, focused time and satisfaction of completing a challenging puzzle.  
However, it can be extremely frustrating if they spend hours searching for a single piece.  

PieceFinder seeks a way to graciously assist puzzlers through a mobile application that accomplishes a few key things:  

1. When a puzzler wants assistance, they can open the app and either take photos of, or open the camera in the app and scan the area they are stuck, scan the available pieces, and the app guides them to try specific pieces believed to be correct.
2. The app must also be able to **count all puzzle pieces** laid out on a table or desk.  
   This helps verify if the puzzle set is complete (e.g., confirming a 1000-piece puzzle really has 1000 pieces).

> **Note:** Eventually, this technology will be the core of the full PieceFinder app, which will also include user profiles, payments, and tracking completed puzzles.  
> However, for this challenge, focus only on the two technical aspects detailed above.

---

## Core Requirements

Your submission must address the following **four components**:

### 1. Research & Evaluation (Technical Landscape Analysis)
**Goal:** Recommend the most suitable open-source computer vision/AI libraries and tools for mobile-based puzzle piece recognition.

**Tasks:**
- Analyze and compare relevant open-source libraries (e.g., Segment Anything, OpenCV, TensorFlow Lite, PyTorch Mobile, etc.).
- Evaluate available pre-trained models for image segmentation and matching.
- Discuss dataset needs and potential sources for training/validation.
- Provide an architecture overview for implementing visual matching in a cross-platform mobile app.
- Justify your technology choices with clear reasoning, scalability considerations, and mobile deployment feasibility.

---

### 2. Puzzle Piece Image Segmentation
**Goal:** Create a preprocessing pipeline to isolate and extract individual puzzle pieces from a photo or live camera feed.

**Tasks:**
- Use open-source segmentation models (e.g., Segment Anything) to identify and label distinct puzzle pieces, even in cluttered or overlapping scenarios.
- Deliver clean, reusable code with example runs and visualizations of segmented results.

---

### 3. Matching Engine
**Goal:** Prototype an AI-powered system to visually match isolated puzzle pieces to empty slots in a puzzle photo.

**Tasks:**
- Use visual features (shape, color, edge continuity) to recommend the top 3 likely matching pieces.
- Return confidence scores or a visual overlay (color-coded or heatmap) to guide the user.
- Optimize for accuracy and speed.

---

### 4. Feedback & Reinforcement Loop
**Goal:** Design a feedback capture mechanism to improve model accuracy over time using user input.

**Tasks:**
- Allow users to mark suggestions as correct or incorrect.
- Incorporate feedback into the model’s learning pipeline (e.g., retraining approach, ranking adjustments).
- Provide a sample data flow diagram for the feedback loop.
- If a suggestion is incorrect, prompt the user to try other highlighted pieces or rescan.


## Deliverables

- Landscape analysis and technology recommendations (report format, PDF or Markdown).
- Well-documented code for segmentation and matching engines, with example runs.
- Visualizations of segmentation and matching results (images or video clips).
- Feedback loop design and sample data flow documentation.
- **Optional:** Demo app or scripts showcasing the entire flow.


## Judging Criteria (in order of importance)

1. **Accuracy & Speed** — Quality of piece-to-slot matching and recommendation speed.
3. **Robustness** — Performance on complex puzzles with high piece counts and unusual shapes.
4. **Real-world Applicability** — Demonstrates it works in realistic conditions (e.g., low light, messy table).
5. **Scalability** — The solution should scale well as the app grows in popularity.
6. **User Experience** — How simple and intuitive the user experience is (e.g., scanning pieces with minimal manual steps).


## Additional Considerations

- Explore whether a user can scan a puzzle box barcode to retrieve the completed puzzle image online for reference.
- Focus on modular, easily-integratable solutions for future mobile app development.
- Clean code and clear documentation are essential.


## Submission Guidelines

Please submit a **zip file** containing the following items:

1. Source code, reports, and documentation on how to run and review the solution.
2. Documentation explaining how the core requirements were fulfilled.
3. A quick video (hosted on Google Drive, accessible link) demonstrating the solution — include the link in the documentation.
