name: Topcoder NestJS Autopilot - Challenge API Integration Fixes
description:
In [the previous challenge](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/30377431), we implemented the Challenge API integration for the Topcoder Autopilot service to automatically transition challenges through their phases. A code review has identified several issues that need to be fixed to ensure the system works as intended. This First2Finish challenge focuses on addressing these issues to improve the reliability and maintainability of the autopilot service.

### Technology Stack

- NestJS 10+
- TypeScript 5+
- @nestjs/schedule module
- Kafka
- Auth0 for authentication
- REST API integration
- ESLint and Prettier for code formatting
- Docker

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git

### Core Requirements

1. **Implement Auth0 Token Generation**

   The current implementation uses a configurable M2M token which can expire. Update the code to:
   
   - Implement an Auth0 service to generate M2M tokens on the fly
   - Use Auth0 configuration (URL, client ID, client secret, domain, audience) for token generation
   - Remove the static M2M token from the configuration
   - Ensure proper error handling and token caching

2. **Fix Challenge API Pagination**

   The current implementation of **getAllActiveChallenges()** doesn't handle pagination, retrieving only the first 20 records. Update the code to:
   
   - Implement pagination handling to retrieve all active challenges
   - Iterate through all pages until empty results are returned
   - Maintain proper error handling and retry mechanisms
   - Update all services that use this method (recovery service, sync service, etc.)

3. **Fix Challenge Identification**

   The current implementation incorrectly uses projectId for filtering challenges and constructing phase keys. Update the code to:
   
   - Replace **c.projectId === projectId** with proper challengeId filtering
   - Update the phaseKey construction to use **${challenge.id}:${phase.id}** instead of using projectId
   - Ensure consistent use of challengeId throughout the codebase
   - Update any affected methods and services

4. **Remove Unnecessary HTTP Headers**

   The current implementation includes unnecessary headers in API requests. Update the code to:
   
   - Remove the User-Agent header from the **getAuthHeader()** method
   - Ensure only necessary headers are included in API requests
   - Maintain proper authentication headers

5. **Rename Misleading Methods**

   The **getActiveChallenge()** method is poorly named as it doesn't check if the challenge is active. Update the code to:
   
   - Rename the method to a more appropriate name (e.g., **getChallengeById()**)
   - Update all references to this method throughout the codebase
   - Ensure method documentation accurately reflects its functionality

6. **Implement Phase Advancement**

   The **advancePhase()** method is implemented but not used anywhere in the codebase. Update the code to:

   - Call this method when a phase transition is triggered
   - Ensure proper error handling and logging
   - Update the scheduler service to use this method when transitioning phases

7. **Fix Docker Compose Configuration**

   The current Docker Compose configuration command "docker compose up -d" generates a warning about an obsolete **version** attribute. 
   
   ```
   docker-compose.yml: the attribute **version** is obsolete, it will be ignored, please remove it to avoid potential confusion
   ```
   
   Update the configuration to:
   
   - Remove the obsolete **version** attribute from docker-compose.yml
   - Ensure the Docker Compose file follows the latest best practices
   - Test that the Docker Compose setup works correctly without warnings

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the **feat/challenge-api-integration** branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git, which should include:

1. Complete implementation of all the fixes listed above
2. Comprehensive tests for the new functionality
3. Updated documentation reflecting the changes

name: TopCoder Lookups API- Fix Review Issues
description:
The Lookups API v6 service has been implemented to provide lookup data for countries, devices, and educational institutions. A code review has identified several issues that need to be addressed to ensure the service meets the requirements. This First2Finish challenge focuses on fixing these issues to improve the quality and functionality of the API.

### Technology Stack

- NestJS 10+
- TypeScript 5+
- PostgreSQL
- Prisma ORM
- Swagger/OpenAPI
- ESLint and Prettier for code formatting
- Docker

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/lookups-api-v6](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/lookups-api-v6)

### Core Requirements

1. **Fix Response Format**

   The API should return responses in the format **{ fromDB: true, result }** but currently returns data directly. Update the code to:
   
   - Implement a consistent response format across all endpoints
   - Wrap all responses in the specified format
   - Ensure proper error handling maintains this format

2. **Remove Unnecessary Response Fields**

   The API is currently returning all fields including **createdAt**, **updatedAt**, and **isDeleted** which should not be exposed. Update the code to:
   
   - Remove these fields from all GET endpoints responses
   - Implement response transformers or DTOs to control the output format
   - Check the v5 API response format at https://api.topcoder.com/v5/lookups/countries for reference

3. **Fix API Base Path**

   The endpoints should be served under the **/v6/lookups** base path. Update the code to:
   
   - Configure the correct base path for all endpoints
   - Update Swagger documentation to reflect the correct API version
   - Ensure all routes are properly configured

4. **Fix Swagger Security Configuration**

   The Swagger security is not mapped correctly, showing all endpoints as secured when some may be accessible without authentication. Update the code to:
   
   - Correctly map security requirements in Swagger documentation
   - Ensure public endpoints are properly documented as such
   - Maintain proper security for protected endpoints

5. **Implement Code Uniqueness Validation**

   The database checks for unique names but not for unique codes. Update the code to:
   
   - Implement uniqueness validation for country codes
   - Add appropriate database constraints in the Prisma schema
   - Add validation in the DTOs for code uniqueness
   - Implement proper error handling for duplicate code attempts

6. **Fix ESLint Configuration**

   The current ESLint configuration has an error with the **globals** package import.

   ```
   Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'globals' imported from /lookups-api-v6/eslint.config.mjs
    Did you mean to import "globals/index.js"?
   ```
   
   Update the code to:
   
   - Fix the ESLint configuration in **eslint.config.mjs**
   - Ensure all required dependencies are properly installed
   - Verify that **npm run lint** executes without errors
   - Maintain code quality standards across the codebase

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the develop branch of the lookups-api-v6 repository, which should include:

1. Complete implementation of all the fixes listed above
2. Updated documentation reflecting the changes (if needed)

name: Topcoder Member API - Legacy Data Import for Member API Migration
description:
This challenge focuses on migrating historical member data from Topcoder's legacy system to the new Prisma-based schema in the Topcoder Member API. In [the previous challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30377016/?type=develop), we successfully migrated the Member API endpoints into the Review API while maintaining backward compatibility. Now, we need to import the legacy submissions data to ensure these endpoints return real data instead of mock responses.

The data to be imported comes from two sources:
1. Legacy Informix database exports (JSON format)
2. ElasticSearch exports from the current Member API

### Technology Stack

- **Backend**: NestJS
- **ORM**: Prisma with PostgreSQL
- **API Documentation**: OpenAPI/Swagger
- **Package Manager**: pnpm

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6)

### Core Requirements:

#### 1. Data Transformation Rules

**1.1 MemberProfile (Dynamo):**

- Convert legacy member records to the new Prisma schema format
- Map member status to the appropriate enum values
- Preserve all metadata including maxRating, addresses, and timestamps
- Ensure proper relationships between member and related entities (memberFinancial, memberHistoryStats, memberStats, memberSkill and memberTraits)

**1.2 MemberStats (Dynamo):**

- Transform MemberStats records to the new schema format
- Ensure proper relationships between MemberStats and related entities (memberMaxRating, memberDevelopStats, memberDataScienceStats, memberCopilotStats and memberDesignStats)

**1.3 MemberStats Private (Dynamo):**

- Transform MemberStats records to the new schema format (model memberStats)
- isPrivate should be true, and should set groupId as well.
- Ensure proper relationships between MemberStats and related entities (memberMaxRating, memberDevelopStats, memberDataScienceStats, memberCopilotStats and memberDesignStats)

**1.4 MemberStatsHistory (Dynamo):**

- Transform MemberStatsHistory records to the new schema format
- Ensure proper relationships between MemberStats and related entities (memberDevelopHistoryStats and memberDataScienceHistoryStats)

**1.5 MemberStatsHistory Private (Dynamo):**

- Transform MemberStatsHistory records to the new schema format
- isPrivate should be true, and should set groupId as well.
- Ensure proper relationships between MemberStats and related entities (memberDevelopHistoryStats and memberDataScienceHistoryStats)

**1.4 ElasticSearch Data:**

- Import data from ElasticSearch exports (members-2020-01.json, memberskills-2020-01.json and memberstats-2020-01.json)
- Map ElasticSearch document fields to the appropriate Prisma schema fields
- Preserve all metadata and relationships

#### 2. Data Integration Rules

**2.1 Handling Overlapping Data:**

- All members, member skills and member stats in ElasticSearch should have a legacy ID that corresponds to a record in the Dynamo data
- Not all Dynamo data will have a corresponding record in ElasticSearch
- When overlaps exist, merge the data appropriately:
  - Use ElasticSearch data for newer fields/properties not present in Dynamo
  - Use Dynamo data as the authoritative source for core fields
  - Preserve unique identifiers from both systems to maintain backward compatibility

**2.2 ID Mapping:**

- Create a mapping between legacy IDs and new auto increased ID
- Ensure all relationships are properly maintained during the migration
- Preserve legacy IDs in the new schema for backward compatibility

#### 3. Validation Requirements

**3.1 Data Quality Checks:**

- Ensure required fields contain valid values
- Validate relationships between each entities
- Confirm data integrity across all imported records
- Verify that ElasticSearch and Dynamo data are properly integrated

**3.2 API Response Validation:**

- All Member API endpoints must return actual database records
- POST/PUT/PATCH operations must persist to migrated data
- Pagination must work with large datasets
- Filter parameters must produce correct results
- Download endpoints must function correctly with the imported data


### Submission Guidelines

Please submit:

1. A git patch against the develop branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6), which should include:
   - Updated Prisma schema if necessary (get approval before making changes)
   - Data migration scripts for both Dynamo and ElasticSearch data
   - Any necessary modifications to existing services to work with the imported data
   - Tests to verify data integrity and API functionality
   - Error handling mechanisms for migration failures

2. Data Migration Script
   - Provide scripts for migrating both Dynamo and ElasticSearch data to the new system
   - Use the official [Prisma seeding](https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/prisma-migrate/workflows/seeding) workflow for data imports
   - JSON-to-Prisma Transformation Logic:
     - Implement logic to transform both data sources into Prisma-compatible formats
     - Handle data merging when records exist in both sources
   - Batch Import with Progress Tracking:
     - Enable batch data imports with a mechanism to track progress
     - Handle the large volume of data efficiently
   - Rollback Mechanism for Failed Imports:
     - Ensure a rollback mechanism is in place to handle failed imports

3. Validation Suite
   - Postman collection testing all Member API endpoints with imported data
   - A document in markdown format with data consistency checks results between legacy/new systems
   - Tests to verify proper integration of data from both sources
   - Tests specifically validating the handling of problematic review records

4. Documentation (in markdown format)
   - Data mapping specification document detailing how fields from both sources map to the new schema
   - Deployment guide
   - Data reconciliation guide explaining how overlaps between data sources were handled

### Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Use the given [GitHub repo's develop branch](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6) as the code base.
- Don't break any existing functionalities of the codebase.
- The goal is to have all Member API endpoints return real data from the migrated legacy system while maintaining backward compatibility.
- Remember that all submissions in ElasticSearch should have a legacy ID that corresponds to a record in the Dynamo data, but not all Dynamo data will have a corresponding record in ElasticSearch.

name: Topcoder Resources API: get full role object for resources API
description:
## Project Overview

The current challenge API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the **resources API** to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

In order to achieve the above goal, we have updated the code in challenge [Topcoder Resources API: Update Code for Prisma Schema and updated Swagger
](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/77910b35-7ad7-4e67-a953-85d88b7d6cf6) and have written Data migration tools in challenge [Topcoder Resources API: Data Migration Tool From Dynamo to Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/f0cc9d2f-e2c4-48f6-9381-ccfb92330773)

The goal for this F2F challenge is to update response so that the full role object(by roleId) is included in this response.

# Individual Requirements

Currently, the getResources API returns only roleId from the response.
For example, https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/resources?challengeId=00fa4ae0-3ac6-4b9b-b3aa-e2765628e48d&memberId=88774619 will return the following:
```json
[
    {
        "id": "0c51eb77-26e7-4b2a-866d-51db760af375",
        "challengeId": "6360c18d-e5fa-4442-a2e1-bf9982acf3b4",
        "memberId": "16096823",
        "memberHandle": "hohosky",
        "roleId": "cfe12b3f-2a24-4639-9d8b-ec86726f76bd",
        "createdBy": "mess",
        "created": "2025-04-01T22:10:07.000Z",
        "rating": 2427
    }
]
```
We should update response of resources APIs so that the full role object(by roleId) is included in this response.
 To be more specificated, we should update getResources API.
# Resources
- Resource API code repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6/tree/develop
- Migration Tool: Will provide in forum

# Submission
Your submission should include:

A patch file against the develop branch of resource API
A comprehensive README that covers the deployment and configuration of the code (if any configuration changes were required)
A validation markdown file that covers how you tested the new functionality

- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger
- Package Manager: pnpm

### Required Links

- Review API (review-api-v6): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git
- GitHub Webhooks Documentation: https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks

### Core Requirements:

1. **Create a New Database Table**

Create a new table named **gitWebhookLog** with the following fields:
- id, type: nanoId, auto generated
- eventId, type: text, not NULL - use the x-github-delivery header value from webhook request
- event, type: text, not NULL - use the x-github-event header value from webhook request
- eventPayload, type: jsonb, not NULL - use to store full webhook event payload
- createdAt, type: timestamp with tz, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

2. **Implement New API Endpoint**

Create a new API endpoint in TC Review API:
- URL: **POST /webhooks/git**
- Security: Each request will be authorized via GitHub's signature verification mechanism
- If authorization fails, request should be rejected with a 403 error

3. **GitHub Webhook Authentication**

Implement GitHub's signature verification mechanism:
- Use the GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET environment variable to store the secret
- Validate the X-Hub-Signature-256 header from GitHub
- Use timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks

4. **Webhook Processing**

Implement webhook processing logic:
- Capture the webhook event
- Log it to the database (create a record in gitWebhookLog)
- Create a handler service that logs the event to console (placeholder for future implementation)
- Respond with 200 OK to the POST request

5. **Documentation**

Create documentation that explains:
- How to generate a GitHub webhook secret
- How to configure the environment variables
- How to set up a webhook in a GitHub repository
- What events are supported

### Implementation Guidelines

1. Ensure all API calls are properly typed using TypeScript interfaces
2. Implement proper error handling for webhook processing
3. Use the existing PrismaService for database operations
4. Use the existing LoggerService for logging

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the develop branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git, which should include:

1. Prisma migration for the new gitWebhookLog table
2. New webhook with controller, service, and guards
4. Documentation on how to set up and use GitHub webhooks with the API

name: Topcoder Identity Service: Tests Improvement
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to rewrite or improve e2e tests, unit tests.

## Detail Requirements
Based on the revised identity service code repository https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop, we should write unit tests and e2e tests thoroughly for the APIs:
- Write E2E tests for each API to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under `test/e2e` folder. 
- Update/Rewrite Unit tests to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under `test/unit` folder.
- All tests should be targeting database data, not just mock. We have a data migration tool  which can be used to import data for tests.
- Log any bugs found during writing tests.
- Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to set up environment and database, how to migrate data, how to deploy locally, how to verify through postman collections;
- Make sure tests code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Patch file of latest commit of develop branch for identity API v6
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

name: Topcoder Review API - Kafka Events Consumer Implementation
description:
In this First2Finish challenge, we need to implement a general Kafka events consumer module in the TC Review API. The module should provide a flexible way to consume Kafka events with configurable topics and handlers, proper logging, and easy integration of new event handlers.

### Technology Stack

- Backend: Node.js with NestJS
- Message Broker: Kafka
- Package Manager: pnpm

### Required Links

- Review API (review-api-v6): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git

### Core Requirements:

1. **Implement KafkaModule**

   Create a new module in the shared modules directory that:
   - Connects to Kafka brokers using configurable settings
   - Provides a flexible way to register and manage event handlers
   - Integrates with the existing application structure

2. **Implement KafkaConsumerService**

   Create a service that:
   - Manages Kafka connections and subscriptions
   - Routes incoming messages to appropriate handlers
   - Provides proper logging before and after handler execution
   - Implements error handling for failed message processing

3. **Implement KafkaHandlerRegistry**

   Create a registry service that:
   - Stores and retrieves event handlers by topic
   - Provides a simple way to register new handlers
   - Returns a list of all registered topics

4. **Implement Base Event Handler**

   Create an abstract base class that:
   - Defines the common interface for all event handlers
   - Provides logging capabilities
   - Standardizes the handler implementation pattern

5. **Implement AVScan Action Scan Handler**

   Create a sample handler that:
   - Subscribes to the 'avscan.action.scan' topic
   - Logs the received event payload
   - Dumps the payload to console

6. **Docker Setup**

Provide Docker configurations that:

- Set up a local development environment
- Include Kafka services
- Enable hot reloading for development
- Configure appropriate environment variables

### Implementation Guidelines

1. Follow NestJS best practices for module and service implementation
2. Use dependency injection for all services
3. Make the Kafka configuration flexible through environment variables
4. Ensure proper error handling and logging throughout the implementation
5. Implement the module in a way that makes adding new event handlers straightforward
6. Integrate with the existing GlobalProvidersModule for application-wide availability

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the develop branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git, which should include:

1. Implementation of the Kafka consumer module
2. Implementation of the AVScan Action Scan handler
3. Updated environment variables sample file
4. Updated package.json with new dependencies
5. Documentation explaining the implementation and how to test it

name: AI Aided Asset Versioning Platform Architecture
description:
### **Challenge Overview**

Marketing teams spend significant time manually adapting creative assets to different channels (Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, etc.) and formats (Story, Feed, Carousel, etc.). This challenge aims to define the system for a platform that uses AI to automate asset resizing, adaptation, and export.

Your task is to design the **database schema**, define the **API interface (Swagger)**, and map key **frontend actions to API operations**. The system will support two components:

* **Admin Panel** for managing platforms, formats, and AI behavior
* **User Panel** for uploading assets and generating adapted variants

### **Tech Stack**
* Python 3
* Postgres
* The underlying AI/LLM must be agnostic — the user can switch to a different LLM easily through configuration (OpenAI, Claude, and Gemini should be supported.)
* React.js


---

### **Functional Scope**

Design: https://marvelapp.com/prototype/11a2f99e/screen/97582714
- Dashboard and Analytics & Usage Report screens are not in scope
- Signup is not in scope
- Forgot password is not in scope
- All other screens are in scope

#### Login Screen
* User can login with username and password, after login, the user can be redirected to admin or user panel based upon user's role.  (No need to have two separate login screen like design)


#### Admin Panel Must Support

* **Platform & Format Management**

  * Add/edit/delete platforms (e.g., Instagram, LinkedIn)
  * Add/edit/delete formats for each platform (e.g., Story, Feed)
  * Set default aspect ratios and resolutions per format
  * Add/edit/delete resizing formats

* **Image Template Rules & Adaptation**

  * Define cropping logic, focal point strategies (e.g., face-centric)
  * Specify overlay and layout zones (logo/text-safe)

* **AI Behavior Controls**

  * Enable/disable manual editing options for users
  * Choose adaptation strategies (crop, expand, add background)
  * Enable/disable manual editing - this option is moved to the "Manual Editing"
  * Select image quality

* **Content Moderation & Upload Rules**

  * Define max file size and accepted formats (Only JPEG and PNG are needed)
  * Flag corrupted or NSFW images

* **Manual Editing**

  * Enable or disable manual editing - if it's enabled, the below options will be displayed. And if it's enabled, the edit mode in user panel is enabled
  * Enable or disable manual cropping
  * Enable or disable color saturation adjustments
  * Enable or disable add text or logo
  * manage allowed logo source
  * manage allowed text style

---

#### User Panel Must Support

* **Upload Workflow**

  * Upload high-resolution assets (e.g., PNG, JPEG)
  * Validate format and size constraints

* **Asset Adaptation**

  * Select desired platforms and formats
  * Edit mode - Manual edit (user can type the text and select the logo etc.) - it's applicable for both resizing and repurposing and it's only enabled if the manual editing is enabled in admin panel
  * Preview variant outputs
  * Trigger batch generation of all selected assets

* **Download & Delivery**

  * Download generated files individually or in bulk (e.g., ZIP)
  * Track status of asset generation jobs



### **Deliverables**

Participants must submit the following items:

1. **Database Schema**

   * Include ERD (diagram) and DDL SQL script
   * Reflects the core entities needed to support admin/user functionality

2. **Swagger/OpenAPI Spec**

   * Complete REST API design for backend operations
   * Use OpenAPI 3.0 or later in YAML or JSON format

3. **UI-to-API Mapping Table**

   * Map all frontend actions to API endpoints
   * Include method, path, parameters, and a brief description per action

4. **Implementation Notes**

   * Describe backend logic, assumptions, and any special behavior
   * Define how AI modules will be triggered and their expected input/output
   * Notes on queue processing, auth, or system integrations

name: Projects API v6 – Core Projects CRUD & Listing
description:
In the [previous challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30377411/?type=develop), we implemented the Schema Definition and TypeScript Skeleton. In this challenge, we aim to implement the core Projects endpoints in the new NestJS/Prisma service, preserving API compatibility with the legacy **tc-project-service** for request/response formats, query parameters (including **fields**), and auth. This builds on Challenge 1’s schema and skeleton.

### Background
The legacy service exposes many endpoints. For v6 we only keep essentials used by Work Manager and reporting, while removing Elasticsearch and flattening JSON columns. This challenge delivers the primary Projects CRUD and list.

### Required Links
- Legacy Projects API: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-project-service
- Projects API v6 repository (develop branch): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/projects-api-v6

### Individual Requirements

#### 1. Database Schema (Prisma)
- Extend the **Project** model to flatten relevant fields from the legacy **details** JSON:
  - **securityRequirements**, **techDependencies**, **techstack**, **apiDefinition**
  - **projectUrl**
- Ensure indexes on commonly filtered/sorted columns (e.g., **status**, **type**, **templateId**, **createdAt**).

#### 2. Endpoints (must match legacy URL patterns and behavior)
- **GET /v5/projects** – list with pagination, sorting, filtering, and **fields** param support
- **POST /v5/projects** – create a new project
- **GET /v5/projects/:projectId** – retrieve a single project
- **PATCH /v5/projects/:projectId** – update project
- **DELETE /v5/projects/:projectId** – soft-delete to match legacy behavior
- **GET /v5/projects/health** – health check

Notes:
- Request validation via DTOs; response formatting via interceptor to preserve legacy response envelope if used
- **fields** query parameter: return only requested top-level properties where supported by legacy (e.g., **description,billingAccountId,attachments** on list/get should behave compatibly; attachments may be empty in this challenge)
- Remove ES dependencies; all data is served from Postgres

#### 3. API Compatibility
- Maintain query param names and semantics: **page**, **perPage**, **sort**, **filter**, **fields**, etc.
- Response shapes must match legacy (property names, casing, optional presence). Where ES-specific fields existed, omit or return empty arrays/objects consistently with legacy fallbacks.

#### 4. Messaging Integration
- Use Bus API client for publishing events only (no direct Kafka integration):
  - **project.action.create** on create
  - **project.action.update** and **project.updated** on update
  - **project.action.delete** and **project.deleted** on delete
- Provide event payload type definitions and unit tests to validate payloads

#### 5. Security & Auth
- JWT-based auth module from Challenge 1
- Authorization parity with legacy roles/scopes for creating/updating/deleting projects (admins, copilots, members)

#### 6. Error Handling & Observability
- Consistent HTTP codes and error bodies with legacy behavior
- Structured logging, request IDs, response timing

### Deliverables

1. Prisma schema updates and migration
2. Controllers, services, DTOs, and mappers for endpoints above
3. Bus API publisher with typed payloads and tests
4. Unit/e2e tests for endpoints, including **fields** support
5. Documentation: endpoint behavior, data model notes, and differences vs. legacy (if any)

### Acceptance Criteria

- All endpoints implemented and passing tests
- **fields** parameter works on list and get (covering at least: **name**, **description**, **status**, **billingAccountId**, **attachments**)
- Events published with correct topics and payloads
- Lint/type checks pass; CI green 

### Final Notes
1. Prioritize simplicity and maintainability while matching legacy API behavior
2. Replace any legacy ES-derived fields with Postgres-only equivalents or omit with parity to legacy fallbacks
3. Keep event payloads minimal but sufficient, documented and typed
4. Add clear migration notes for new flattened columns
5. Should be lint error free and fix the existing lint errors.

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the develop branch of the projects-api-v6 repository, which should include:

1. Prisma **schema.prisma** changes and migration files for flattened fields
2. NestJS modules, controllers, services, and DTOs for: **GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /v5/projects**, **GET /v5/projects/:projectId**, **GET /v5/projects/health**
3. Bus API integration code and typed event payload definitions for project create/update/delete and **project.action.*** topics
4. Unit/e2e tests, including **fields** param support and response compatibility
5. Documentation updates (API behavior, data model notes, migration instructions)

name: Topcoder Challenge API - Add field for "wiproAllowed"
description:
### Project overview

The current challenge  API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the challenge API to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

In order to achieve the above goal, we have updated the code in challenge [Topcoder Challenge  API: Update Code for Prisma Schema and updated Swagger](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/7dc44b10-2e27-4e57-bec1-4891c34aec4a) and have written Data migration tools in challenge [Topcoder Challenge API: Data Migration Tool to Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/22477223-2354-449d-a3b5-2d4d63c4cb25)

The goal for this challenge is to Add field `wiproAllowed`.

### Individual Requirements

We need a field to indicate whether Wipro employees are allowed to compete in a challenge:
- We’ll need a new `wiproAllowed` flag in the root of the challenge object and stored in Postgres. 
- For all past challenges, we’ll just mark this as True, and going forward the default will be False. You need to do changes in the migration tools to set `wiproAllowed` to True for past challenges.
- This won’t be a required field - if data isn’t provided for this, it will always just default to False.

### Code

- Challenge API code repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/tree/develop

### Unit tests

Unit tests are not in scope for this challenge, but any existing tests are expected to pass as they do now - we can't break any tests that are currently passing.

### Code quality
Please make sure you're following the code base quality & standards. Do not refactor/modify or start a new code styling. 

### Submission

* A patch file against the develop branch of Challenge API, hash `d4d114e2795bb18d9d323adde8e2ffb01d2aec4c` or later
* A comprehensive README that covers the deployment and configuration of the code (if any configuration changes were required)
* A validation markdown file that covers how you tested the new project functionality

name: 72 hours Topcoder Review API Gitea Workflow Triggers for Submission Scan Complete
description:
**Note: this is a fast challenge with shorter submission phase, review phase** 

This challenge implements an event-driven integration that listens to the Kafka event **submission.scan.complete** and triggers configured Gitea workflows for the associated challenge. The logic will be encapsulated in a dedicated service and wired through the existing Kafka handler entry point in the Review API codebase. It must use the already integrated Submissions module to fetch submission details and the existing challenge service to fetch challenge metadata. Gitea will be accessed with a token and a generated TypeScript client based on its Swagger spec.

### Technology Stack

- **Backend**: NestJS
- **Package Manager**: pnpm
- **Messaging**: Kafka
- **API Client Generation**: OpenAPI/Swagger (TypeScript client)
- **Auth**: Token-based for Gitea
- **Code Style/Tooling**: ESLint, Prettier

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6)
- Entry Point: **review-api-v6/src/shared/modules/kafka/handlers/submission-scan-complete.handler.ts**
- Challenge Service: **review-api-v6/src/shared/modules/global/challenge.service.ts**
- Submissions Endpoint: **review-api-v6/src/api/submission/submission.controller.ts**
- Gitea Swagger: [https://git.topcoder-dev.com/api/swagger](https://git.topcoder-dev.com/api/swagger)
- Gitea Organization: [https://git.topcoder-dev.com/TC-Reviews-Tests](https://git.topcoder-dev.com/TC-Reviews-Tests)

### Core Requirements

#### 1. Services and Abstractions

- **1.1 Submissions Base Service**
  - Create a global base class service (e.g., **SubmissionBaseService**) with:
    - **getSubmissionById(id: string)**
  - Implementation detail: call into the internal Submissions module/service (Prisma-backed) via dependency injection. Do not make external HTTP calls or use M2M tokens.

- **1.2 Gitea Base Service**
  - Create a global base class service (e.g., **GiteaService**) that:
    - Uses a generated TypeScript client from Gitea's Swagger to interact with the API.
    - Reads a personal access token (will be provided in the forum) from environment variables for authentication.
  - Responsibilities:
    - Check if a repository named exactly as the **challengeId** exists in a configured organization.
    - Create the repository if it does not exist.
    - Dispatch one or more workflows using the **ActionsDispatchWorkflow** API, passing the workflow ID, ref, and parameters.

- **1.3 Dedicated Orchestration Service**
  - Implement a dedicated service (e.g., **SubmissionScanCompleteOrchestrator**) to encapsulate the end-to-end flow. Do not place logic directly in the Kafka handler.
  - This service will:
    - Filter out submissions where **isInfected** is true.
    - Fetch submission details by **submissionId** from the event via **SubmissionBaseService**.
    - Fetch challenge details from **getChallengeDetail** in **challenge.service.ts** using **challengeId** from the submission.
    - Ensure the Gitea repository exists for the challenge, creating it if needed.
    - Iterate through an array of configured workflows for the challenge and call **ActionsDispatchWorkflow** for each.

#### 2. Event Handling Flow

- **2.1 Kafka Handler**
  - Wire the **submission.scan.complete** Kafka handler to invoke **SubmissionScanCompleteOrchestrator**
  - The handler should only parse and validate the event payload, then delegate the processing.

- **2.2 Event Payload Assumptions**
  - Payload includes at minimum: **submissionId: string**, **isInfected: boolean** (or equivalent field), and metadata needed to correlate to a challenge (derivable from submission details).
  - If **isInfected** is true, skip processing and log a structured info message.

- **2.3 Workflow Configuration Model**
  - Support an array of workflow configurations attached to a challenge. For this challenge, use mock/static mapping as source of truth (to be refined later):
    ```
    [
      {
        workflowId: "abc1234",
        ref: "refs/heads/main",
        params: {
          key: "value"
          // ...many parameters mapped to Gitea API inputs
        }
      }
    ]
    ```
  - Implement robust iteration and error isolation so that one failing workflow does not prevent others from being dispatched.

#### 3. Gitea API Integration

- **3.1 Client Generation**
  - Generate a TypeScript client from **https://git.topcoder-dev.com/api/swagger**.
  - Integrate the generated client into the codebase in a suitable location (e.g., **src/shared/clients/gitea**).
  - The generation step should be reproducible via a script or documented command.

- **3.2 Authentication**
  - Use a Gitea token from environment variables (e.g., **GITEA_TOKEN**).
  - Include base URL and organization name as environment variables (e.g., **GITEA_BASE_URL**, **GITEA_ORG**, **challengeId**).

- **3.3 Repository Handling**
  - Check existence of repo named **challengeId** within **GITEA_ORG**.
  - If not found, create it with sensible defaults (visibility, README, branch protections optional in this phase).
  - In the future, the repo will be created during a challenge creation. The point is to have a repo setup with the workflows.

- **3.4 Workflow Dispatch**
  - Call **ActionsDispatchWorkflow** for each configured workflow with the following:
    - **workflow_id** (string)
    - **ref** (string)
    - **inputs/params** (object)
  - Log the dispatch result IDs/metadata for traceability.

#### 4. Submissions Module Integration (Internal)

- **4.1 Internal Access**
  - Use the Submissions module's service layer (Prisma-backed) to implement **getSubmissionById(id: string)** via dependency injection.
  - Rely on the existing endpoint and service that powers **GET /api/submission/:submissionId** internally, avoiding any external API calls.

- **4.2 Required Fields**
  - Ensure the returned submission model includes at minimum: **id**, **challengeId**, **isInfected** (or equivalent malware scan status field), and any additional metadata needed by workflow params.

#### 5. Configuration and Environment

- **5.1 Environment Variables**
  - **KAFKA_BROKERS**, **KAFKA_GROUP_ID** (reuse existing if present)
  - **GITEA_BASE_URL**, **GITEA_TOKEN**, **GITEA_ORG**

- **5.2 Local Development**
  - Provide a **.env.example** reflecting all required variables.
  - Ensure **docker-compose.kafka.yml** remains compatible; update docs if any changes are needed.

#### 6. Error Handling, Logging, and Observability

- **6.1 Error Handling**
  - Gracefully handle API failures (Gitea) and internal service errors with retries where appropriate and clear error messages.
  - If a single workflow dispatch fails, continue with the remaining workflows and aggregate/report errors.

- **6.2 Logging**
  - Use structured logs that include **submissionId**, **challengeId**, and **workflowId**.
  - Log at info level for normal operations and at warn/error for failures.

- **6.3 Metrics (Optional/Nice-to-Have)**
  - Counters for dispatched workflows, failures, and skipped (infected) submissions.

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit:

1. A git patch against the **feat/ai-workflows** branch of **https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git** including:
   - New services: **SubmissionBaseService**, **GiteaService**, and **SubmissionScanCompleteOrchestrator**.
   - Kafka handler wiring at **src/shared/modules/kafka/handlers/submission-scan-complete.handler.ts** delegating to the orchestrator.
   - Generated Gitea TypeScript client and integration code.
   - Environment variable plumbing and **.env.example** updates.
   - Scripts/documentation to regenerate the Gitea client.

2. Configuration and Scripts
   - A reproducible command/script to generate the Gitea TS client from Swagger (document any required tools).
   - Optional: a small producer script to emit a **submission.scan.complete** event to Kafka locally for testing.

3. Documentation (markdown)
   - Developer guide describing architecture, data/control flow, and how to run locally.
   - Deployment guide including environment variables and any infrastructure assumptions.
   - Troubleshooting guide for common failures (auth, repo creation, workflow dispatch).

### Implementation Notes

- Keep business logic out of the Kafka handler; the handler should delegate to the orchestrator service.
- Use dependency injection consistent with the NestJS module architecture in the repository.
- Ensure idempotency where possible (e.g., repository creation checks before attempts).
- Use clear, typed interfaces for workflow configuration objects.
- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.

name: 72 Hours Topcoder Review App Challenge Review Details Page Integration Part 2
description:
In the [previous challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30377544/?type=develop), we integrated the APIs for the header section, registration tab, contact manager and forum link, this challenge aims to complete the remaining parts of the Challenge Review Details page by integrating real APIs for the following tabs:
- Submission / Screening
- Winners
- Review / Appeals

Use the provided APIs, remove mock data, and wire the UI to live endpoints.

### Technology Stack

- Frontend
  - React + TypeScript
  - SCSS + CSS Modules
  - SWR for data fetching and caching

### Assets

- Review App (platform-ui): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/platform-ui
- Review API (review-api-v6)
  - Source code: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git
  - Hosted version: https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/review
- Challenge API (challenge-api-v6):
  - Source code: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6.git
  - Hosted version: https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/challenges
- Resource API (resource-api-v6):
  - Source code: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6.git
  - Hosted version: https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/resources

### Timeline

- This is a fast challenge
- Submission Phase: 72 hours
- Review Phase: 24 hours

### General Requirements

- Use SWR hooks for all data fetching, with proper caching and revalidation
- Type all responses with accurate TypeScript interfaces based on DTOs
- Handle loading and error states; show centered "No records found" when empty
- Respect role-based behavior (Reviewer, Copilot, Submitter) as per the UI logic
- Remove any mock data and feature-flagged placeholders related to these tabs

### Tab Integrations

#### 1. Submission / Screening Tab

Goal: Display submissions for a challenge and their screening info.

Endpoints:
- List submissions
  - GET Review API: **/v6/review/api/submissions**
  - Query params supported (see **SubmissionQueryDto**):
    - **challengeId**: string (use the current **challengeId**)
    - **type**, **legacySubmissionId**, **legacyUploadId**, **submissionPhaseId**, **url** (optional)
  - Auth: roles Admin, Copilot, Submitter, and Reviewer with scope **read:submission**
- Download submission file
  - GET Review API: **/v6/review/api/submissions/:submissionId/download**
  - Auth: roles Admin, Copilot, Submitter, Reviewer with scope **read:submission**
- List artifacts (if needed by the UI)
  - GET Review API: **/v6/review/api/submissions/:submissionId/artifacts**
- Download artifact
  - GET Review API: **/v6/review/api/submissions/:submissionId/artifacts/:artifactId/download**

Data Model references:
- **SubmissionResponseDto** (Review API)
  - Includes fields like **id**, **type**, **memberId**, **challengeId**, **submittedDate**, and optional **review**/**reviewSummation**
- Screening indicators
  - Screening-related enums: **SubmissionStatus.FAILED_SCREENING**, **ScorecardType.SCREENING**
  - The Reviews endpoint exposes review items and comments; use those to surface screening feedback if required by the UI

UI Requirements:
- Fetch submissions via SWR with **challengeId**
- Show handle, submission ID, submitted date/time, and links to download the submission/artifacts where applicable
- Surface screening status where available
- Empty state: show "No records found"
- Loading: use existing **TableLoading**

#### 2. Winners Tab

Goal: Show winners and placements for the challenge.

Endpoint:
- GET Review API: **/v6/review/projectResult?challengeId=:challengeId**
  - Controller: **ProjectResultController.getProjectResults**
  - Roles: Reviewer, Copilot, and Submitter with scope **read:project-result**
  - Returns a paginated list of **ProjectResultResponseDto**

Data Model reference:
- **ProjectResultResponseDto** fields include:
  - **challengeId**, **userId**, **submissionId**, **placement**, **initialScore**, **finalScore**, **rated**, **passedReview**, **validSubmission**, and nested **reviews** with **appeals**

UI Requirements:
- Fetch project results via SWR with **challengeId**
- Display placements, handles, scores, and the reviewer list (as per current UI)
- Empty state: "No records found"
- Loading state is supported

#### 3. Review / Appeals Tab

Use the same submissions data from the Submission / Screening Tab; the score and appeals data can be dummy for now.

#### Shared/Supporting

- Challenge phases are already available via the Challenge API:
  - GET Challenge: **/v6/challenges/:challengeId**
  - Phases include Screening, Review, Appeals, and Appeals Response; use these for gating actions
- Registrants list for handle lookups can be sourced via the Resource API if needed:
  - GET **/v6/resources?challengeId=:challengeId**

### Authorization & Headers

- Use the user JWT in the browser; ensure that endpoints requiring roles/scopes are called only when the user has access
- In production, the Review API runs under the **/v6/review** prefix; in dev, it may be root. Keep the base URL configurable via the environment

### Important Notes

- Submission / Screening tab pulls real submissions and shows download links, and correctly handles empty and loading states
- Winners tab fetches **ProjectResultResponseDto** and renders placements, final scores, and the reviewers' section
- Use SWR hooks for data fetching and caching across all API integrations.
- All components must be responsive and work properly on mobile devices.
- Implement appropriate error handling and "no data" states for all components.
- Remove any related mock data.
- All role and scope restrictions are respected on the UI
- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.

### Submission Guidelines

Your submission should include:

1. Git patches against the specified branch in the repository:
   - platform-ui: the feat/review branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/platform-ui 

2. Documentation (in markdown) explaining:
   - How the Review API is integrated into the Challenge Review Details page
   - How to test all implemented features

name: Topcoder Identity Service Tests Improvement Part 1
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to rewrite or improve e2e tests, unit tests and this is the first part which focuses on **RoleController**, **UserController**.

## Detail Requirements
Based on the revised identity service code repository https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop, we should write unit tests and e2e tests thoroughly for **RoleController, UserController APIs and related services RoleService, AuthFlowService, TwoFactorAuthService, UserProfileService, UserService, and ValidationService**:
- Write E2E tests for each APIs to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under **test/e2e** folder. You can refer to [Challenge.api.test.js](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/blob/develop/test/e2e/challenge.api.test.js).
- Update/Rewrite Unit tests for each services to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under **test/unit** folder.
- All Unit Tests should be targeting database data, not just mock. You can refer to [ChallengeService.test.js](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/blob/develop/test/unit/ChallengeService.test.js). We have a data migration tool  which can be used to import data for tests.
- Log any bugs found during writing tests.
- Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to set up environment and database, how to migrate data, how to deploy locally, how to verify through postman collections;
- Make sure tests code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Patch file of latest commit of develop branch for identity API v6
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

name: Learn AI Build and Deploy an AI Agent with Topcoder MCP on Hugging Face
description:
Welcome to the Learn AI Challenge series\! Your mission is to explore AI and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) by building an innovative AI Agent application that leverages the Topcoder MCP server.

The agent can take many forms—chatbot, automation tool, research assistant, code generator, data visualization tool, or something entirely unique.

You are free to use open-source agents or develop your own from scratch. The only requirement is that the agent must utilize the Topcoder MCP server and deliver a meaningful, user-friendly experience through the server's tools and resources.

This is your opportunity to learn, innovate, and demonstrate your creativity and engineering expertise using cutting-edge AI technologies—plus earn rewards and community recognition.

## **Challenge Incentives**

* Top 10 winners will receive cash prizes.
* Winners and top submissions will be awarded an "AI Explorer" badge on their Topcoder profiles.
* Selected high-quality submissions will be featured on the [Topcoder Hugging Face organization page](https://huggingface.co/Topcoder-Platform), providing recognition and exposure to the broader AI community.

## **Challenge Details**

**Technology Focus:** Development of AI Agents using the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
**Submission Duration:** 21 days
**Agent Type:** Any form of intelligent application is allowed—such as chatbots, virtual assistants, research tools, code generators, or data visualization agents. 
**Creativity:** Participants are encouraged to push boundaries and design original, inventive AI applications.
**Functionality:** The AI agent must address a clearly defined problem or purpose and deliver impactful, practical results.
**Implementation Requirements:**
* The AI agent must be designed to run within a [Hugging Face Space](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/spaces-overview).
* You can use any SDK or programming language, as long as it functions correctly in the Hugging Face Space environment.
* The agent must run smoothly on the default free-tier hardware (CPU Basic) provided by Hugging Face—no GPU usage required.
* It must connect to and interact with the Topcoder MCP server and deliver clear, purposeful results through an intuitive and user-friendly interface.

## **Submission Requirements**

* A fully functional AI Agent application, either as a downloadable codebase (zip file) or a publicly accessible Hugging Face Space link—or both.
* The submission must be based on a clearly defined use case that addresses a specific problem. Participants should also include a simple, clear document (as part of the submission) that outlines the problem being solved, the rationale behind the chosen use case, and how the AI agent addresses it. This clarity should also be reflected in the agent’s implementation and user experience.
* Accompanying documentation that clearly explains how to deploy, configure, and run the AI agent on Hugging Face Spaces.
* An optional demo video (up to 5 minutes) showcasing the agent’s core functionality and how it addresses the defined use case.

## **What You Will Learn**

By participating in this challenge, you will:

* Gain a practical understanding of AI agent architecture, development workflows, and prompt engineering techniques.
* Strengthen your hands-on skills using technologies such as TypeScript, Python, JSON, APIs, and Gradio in real-world scenarios.
* Learn to build, integrate, and test AI applications using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server provided by Topcoder.
* Develop proficiency in working with data ingestion, backend logic, and designing clear and effective user experiences (UI/UX) for AI tools.
* Learn how to prepare, optimize, and deploy your application to Hugging Face Spaces using their CPU Basic environment.
* Improve your ability to define clear use cases, articulate the problem your solution addresses, and communicate that effectively through documentation and demonstration.
* Engage with the broader Topcoder AI community to gain visibility, collect feedback, and earn recognition through badges, publications, or prizes.
* Build portfolio-worthy work that demonstrates your ability to solve meaningful problems using modern AI tools.

## **Learning Path & Plan**

This 3-week challenge is structured as a guided learning journey. Below is a comprehensive, curriculum-style blueprint divided into modules, each with a clear objective, tasks, and deliverables. This will help you progress from beginner to deployer by the end of the challenge.

### **Module 1: Foundations of AI Agents & MCP (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Understand the core principles of AI agents and how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables real-world AI integration.

* What are AI agents? (Examples: chatbots, automation tools, assistants, research agents)
* Core capabilities of agents: statefulness, prompt context, responsiveness, and output generation
* Overview of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and how it facilitates structured communication between users and AI models
* Learn about transports like SSE and Streamable HTTP used by MCP
* Understand how MCP connects to Topcoder’s public APIs and what kind of tools/data you can access

* **Recommended Course:** [Model Context Protocol (MCP) Course by Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/learn/mcp-course/unit0/introduction#welcome-to-the--model-context-protocol-mcp-course)

  * Covers how to build MCP clients
  * Shows integration with Gradio
  * Includes code walkthroughs, examples, and deployment steps

* **Deliverable:** Written summary explaining:  
  * The type of AI agent you want to build  
  * How MCP can be used in that context

### **Module 2: Development Environment Setup (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Get your local environment ready to build, test, and run MCP-powered AI agents.

* Install and configure a suitable IDE (e.g., VSCode, Cursor)
* Set up the Topcoder MCP server endpoint (`https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp`) in your IDE’s `settings.json`
* Install relevant SDKs and dependencies (e.g., TypeScript, Python, Gradio, Node.js, etc.)
* Use the MCP Inspector to validate server connection and test a basic query

**Recommended Resources:**

* [Gradio Getting Started Guide (with code examples and UI integrations)](https://www.gradio.app/guides)
* [VSCode Official Setup Instructions](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs), [Cursor Editor (AI-powered IDE)](https://www.cursor.so/)
* [Topcoder MCP Inspector GitHub](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector)

**Deliverable:**

* A working development environment
* Screenshot or confirmation of successful test interaction with MCP server using MCP Inspector

### **Module 3: Exploring MCP Server Capabilities (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Learn how to explore the Topcoder MCP server, understand available tools and data, and identify what can power your agent.

* Understand the difference between SSE and Streamable HTTP transports in MCP.
* Launch the MCP Inspector and connect it to the Topcoder MCP endpoint (`https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp`).
* Explore the public tools and resources available via the server.
* Review example requests and responses, and document the ones relevant to your intended use case.
* Determine the input and output formats for at least one useful endpoint.

**Recommended Resources:**

* [Topcoder MCP Inspector GitHub](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector)
* [SSE and HTTP Streaming \- MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events)

**Deliverable:**

* A table listing at least 3 MCP tools or endpoints with their purpose, input, and expected output
* Screenshots or logs of actual request/response payloads from the MCP Inspector

### **Module 4: Refine the Use Case & Design the Agent (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Revisit your original agent idea after exploring the Topcoder MCP server and finalize a well-defined use case and experience flow.

* Reflect on your initial idea from Module 1 in light of the MCP tools and data you've explored
* Validate whether the original use case still makes sense or needs to be adapted
* Clearly define:
  * The real-world problem you're solving
  * Why it's meaningful
  * How your agent will solve it using one or more MCP tools
* Create a user journey flow, wireframe, or basic mockup to visualize how users will interact with your agent

**Deliverable:**

* Finalized use case document with:
  * Problem definition
  * Target user or audience
  * Description of solution and value proposition
  * MCP tools or endpoints your agent will use
* UX mockup or user journey diagram that reflects the agent's intended experience. You can use [v0.dev](https://v0.dev/) to quickly generate visually appealing mockups with the help of AI.

### **Module 5: Building Agent Logic & MCP Integration (Days 2–3)**

**Objective:** Implement the core logic of your agent to communicate effectively with the Topcoder MCP server, retrieve data, and generate intelligent outputs.

* Set up your project structure using a preferred language (Python, TypeScript, etc.)
* Choose your connection method (SSE or Streamable HTTP) and implement a request handler for MCP
* Integrate the MCP endpoint(s) you selected in Module 3
* Process incoming data and prepare meaningful responses
* Apply prompt engineering principles to enhance agent output quality
* Ensure your logic runs efficiently on Hugging Face CPU Basic (no GPU dependencies)

**Recommended Resources:**

* [Prompt Engineering Guide](https://github.com/dair-ai/Prompt-Engineering-Guide)
* Example backend agent using Python with SSE \+ Hugging Face: [MCP Agent Boilerplate (Python)](https://github.com/lastmile-ai/mcp-agent)
  * Demonstrates how to:
    * Connect to Topcoder MCP using SSE
    * Build an async backend agent using FastAPI
    * Deploy to Hugging Face with CPU basic config
  * This is ideal if you're building a backend-only agent with no frontend SDK involved.
* EventSource API Reference: [MDN Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/EventSource) Guide\]([https://github.com/dair-ai/Prompt-Engineering-Guide](https://github.com/dair-ai/Prompt-Engineering-Guide))
* Example SSE integration (React or Node): [MDN EventSource Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/EventSource)

**Deliverable:**

* A backend agent logic module that:
  * Successfully connects to and queries the MCP server
  * Returns usable responses to the frontend
  * Demonstrates use of at least one real MCP tool or endpoint
* A brief explanation (in your README) of how the MCP connection is implemented and which tools it uses

### **Module 6: UI/UX Development (Days 2–3)**

**Objective:** Create an accessible, intuitive interface to showcase your AI agent’s capabilities.

* Choose a simple UI framework such as Gradio, Streamlit, or a minimal HTML/JS frontend (Gradio recommended for ease of Hugging Face integration)
* Link the UI to your backend logic (built in Module 5\)
* Focus on making input fields and output displays clear and accessible
* Provide contextual labels, input placeholders, and output formatting where helpful
* Consider adding a loading indicator or progress feedback if MCP requests take time
* 
**Deliverable:** A lightweight, working UI prototype that allows users to interact with your agent and see live responses from the MCP server

### **Module 7: End-to-End Testing & Debugging (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Ensure your agent is production-ready through comprehensive testing and optimization.

* Perform full end-to-end testing: UI → backend → MCP → backend → UI
* Simulate real user interactions and input variations
* Identify and handle edge cases, unexpected input, and communication errors (e.g., timeouts, SSE disconnects)
* Add fallback behavior or helpful messages for errors
* Profile and optimize response time, agent feedback, and loading states

**Deliverable:** A stable, polished, and bug-free agent that reliably handles diverse inputs and edge scenarios

### **Module 8: Deployment on Hugging Face Spaces (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Deploy your agent to a live environment and ensure it's accessible and stable.

* Prepare `requirements.txt` and `README.md` with necessary dependencies and setup instructions
* Push your project to a new or existing Hugging Face Space
* Confirm successful deployment on the default CPU Basic hardware (no GPU)
* Perform smoke testing to validate end-to-end functionality

**Deliverable:** A fully deployed Hugging Face Space (public link) or a repository that’s ready to be deployed without additional changes

### **Module 9: Documentation & Demo (Days 1–2)**

**Objective:** Ensure your project is easy to understand, use, and present.

* Create a well-structured README that includes setup instructions, usage examples, and technical details
* Clearly articulate the use case and the problem your agent addresses
* (Optional) Record a 3–5 minute demo video walking through how your agent works

**Deliverable:** Comprehensive documentation with an optional demo video

### **Module 10: Final Packaging & Submission (Day 1\)**

**Objective:** Finalize and submit your agent project with all required materials.

* Package your project as a zip file or provide a live public link (e.g., Hugging Face Space)
* Carefully review the challenge submission checklist to ensure everything is included
* Submit your entry via the challenge platform before the deadline

**Deliverable:** A complete submission package that meets all criteria and is ready for judging

## **The Topcoder MCP Server**

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) server provided by Topcoder is hosted at: [https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp).

It supports two transport mechanisms for agent communication:
* **Server-Sent Events (SSE):** [https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/sse](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/sse)
* **Streamable HTTP:** [https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp)

Your agent must use one of these transports to interact with the MCP server.
No authentication is currently required—your agent can connect and send requests without needing a token.
The server exposes publicly available data and tools sourced from the Topcoder APIs. In future updates, we may introduce authenticated features and enhanced tools that require login or user tokens.

## **Review & Evaluation**

We will review submissions on an **iterative basis**. After completing each module, please upload your work (e.g., Document, link to GitHub repo, Hugging Face Space, or zipped files) and notify the challenge copilot by email, the copilot will reivew and share feedback over email.

You will typically receive feedback within **1–2 days**, helping you improve and refine your work before progressing.

This **optional collaborative review** process ensures you stay on track and gives you the best chance of submitting a high-quality agent by the end.

## **Final Evaluation Criteria**

Your final submission will be judged based on the following:

* **Functionality & Stability (30%)** – Does the agent work as intended across different inputs and edge cases?
* **Use Case Relevance (20%)** – How clearly defined and meaningful is the problem the agent addresses?
* **MCP Integration (20%)** – How effectively does the agent leverage the Topcoder MCP tools and transports?
* **User Experience & Interface (15%)** – Is the UI intuitive, responsive, and polished?
* **Documentation & Demo (10%)** – Is the README clear? Does the optional video explain the agent effectively?
* **Originality & Creativity (5%)** – Is the idea novel or uniquely executed?

High-scoring submissions typically demonstrate smooth functionality, a clear purpose, practical use of MCP tools, and thoughtful design.

name: 72 Hours Topcoder Review App Challenge Review Edit Page Integration
description:
This challenge focuses on integrating real APIs for the Challenge Review Edit Page (ScorecardDetailsPage) and updating the Review / Appeals Tab in the Challenge Review Details page. Currently, both features use mock data and need to be connected to live Review API endpoints for reviews, appeals, and appeal responses management.

The main objectives are:
- Replace mock data in **ScorecardDetailsPage** with real Review API integration
- Implement full CRUD operations for appeals and appeal responses
- Update the Review / Appeals Tab to display actual score and appeals data
- Ensure proper role-based access control and phase gating

### Technology Stack

- Frontend
  - React + TypeScript
  - SCSS + CSS Modules
  - SWR for data fetching and caching
  - React Hook Form for form management

### Assets

- Review App (platform-ui): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/platform-ui
- Review API (review-api-v6)
  - Source code: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git
  - Hosted version: https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/review
- Challenge API (challenge-api-v6):
  - Source code: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6.git
  - Hosted version: https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/challenges

### Timeline

- This is a fast challenge
- Submission Phase: 72 hours
- Review Phase: 24 hours

### General Requirements

- Use SWR hooks for all data fetching, with proper caching and revalidation
- Type all responses with accurate TypeScript interfaces based on Review API DTOs
- Handle loading and error states appropriately
- Respect role-based behavior (Reviewer, Copilot, Admin, Submitter) as per the UI logic
- Remove any mock data and feature-flagged placeholders
- Implement proper phase gating using Challenge API phase information
- Maintain existing UI/UX patterns and responsive design

### Primary Integration Tasks

#### 1. ScorecardDetailsPage API Integration

**Goal:** Replace mock data in the scorecard details page with real Review API calls and implement appeals management functionality.

**Current State:** 
- Uses **useFetchReviews** hook with mock data from **fetchReviewInfo**, **fetchAppeals**, **fetchScorecards**
- Has UI components for appeals but limited real API integration

**Endpoints:**

**Reviews:**

- GET **/v6/review/reviews?challengeId=:challengeId&submissionId=:submissionId**
  - Returns **ReviewResponseDto[]** including **reviewItems** and **reviewItemComments**
  - Roles: Reviewer, Copilot, Admin, Submitter with scope **read:review**
- POST **/v6/review/reviews** (if creating new reviews)
  - Body: **ReviewRequestDto**
  - Roles: Reviewer with scope **create:review**
- PATCH **/v6/review/reviews/:reviewId** (if updating reviews)
  - Body: **ReviewRequestDto**
  - Roles: Reviewer with scope **update:review**

**Appeals:**

- GET **/v6/review/appeals?challengeId=:challengeId&reviewId=:reviewId&resourceId=:resourceId**
  - Returns **AppealResponseDto[]** with optional **appealResponse** 
  - Roles: Admin, Copilot with scope **read:appeal**; Submitter can read their own appeals
- POST **/v6/review/appeals**
  - Body: **AppealRequestDto** (challengeId, reviewId, reviewItemCommentId, content)
  - Roles: Submitter with scope **create:appeal**
- PATCH **/v6/review/appeals/:appealId**
  - Body: **AppealRequestDto**
  - Roles: Submitter with scope **update:appeal**
- DELETE **/v6/review/appeals/:appealId**
  - Roles: Submitter with scope **delete:appeal**

**Appeal Responses:**
- POST **/v6/review/appeals/:appealId/response**
  - Body: **AppealResponseRequestDto** (content, success)
  - Roles: Reviewer with scope **create:appeal-response**
- PATCH **/v6/review/appeals/response/:appealResponseId**
  - Body: **AppealResponseRequestDto**
  - Roles: Reviewer with scope **update:appeal-response**

**UI Requirements:**

**For Non-Submitter Roles (Reviewer/Copilot/Admin):**
- Display reviews with items and comments in read-only or edit mode
- Show existing appeals attached to review item comments
- During Appeals Response phase: Allow Reviewers to create/update appeal responses
- Display appeal response status (success/failure) clearly

**For Submitter Role:**
- During Appeals phase: Allow creating/updating/deleting appeals against review item comments
- Show appeal status and responses from reviewers
- Prevent submitter access to edit mode (already implemented)

**Phase Gating:**
- Use Challenge API phase information to determine current phase
- Appeals creation/editing: only during Appeals phase for Submitters
- Appeal responses: only during Appeals Response phase for Reviewers
- Show appropriate messaging when actions are not allowed due to phase restrictions

#### 2. Review / Appeals Tab Integration

Update the Review / Appeals Tab in **ChallengeDetailsContent** to show real score and appeals data instead of mock data.

**UI Requirements:**
- Replace mock **SubmissionInfo.reviews** with real review data from API
- Show actual appeal counts per submission: **[successful appeals / total appeals]**
- Display real reviewer handles, scores, and timestamps
- Update links to scorecard details to pass correct IDs
- Maintain existing responsive design and mobile layout
- Handle empty states properly when no reviews/appeals exist

### Important Notes

- Use SWR caching to minimize redundant API calls
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing UI components
- Preserve all existing role-based access control logic
- Ensure phase-based restrictions are properly enforced
- Remove all related mock data files and references
- Test thoroughly with different user roles and challenge phases
- All components must remain responsive and work properly on mobile devices
- Use the existing confirmation modals and error handling patterns
- Maintain consistent naming conventions and code organization

### Submission Guidelines

Your submission should include:

1. Git patches against the specified branch in the repository:
   - platform-ui: the feat/review branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/platform-ui 

2. Documentation (in markdown) explaining:
   - How the Review API endpoints are integrated into the ScorecardDetailsPage
   - How appeals and appeal responses CRUD operations work
   - How the Review/Appeals Tab is updated to show real data
   - How to test all implemented features with different user roles
   - Any environment configuration required for the Review API integration

3. Updated TypeScript interfaces and service layer documentation
4. Testing instructions for different user roles and challenge phases
